instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCISBUSCOUPLER property do?
output: The FUNC_PLCISBUSCOUPLER property identifies a device as a bus coupler or head station. In the case of a head station, the Rack property has to be filled additionally for the respective PLC card. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCISBUSCOUPLER property?
output: The FUNC_PLCISBUSCOUPLER property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCISBUSDISTRIBUTOR property identify?
output: The FUNC_PLCISBUSDISTRIBUTOR property identifies a PLC box as a bus distribution device. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCISBUSDISTRIBUTOR property?
output: The FUNC_PLCISBUSDISTRIBUTOR property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCISCPU property identify?
output: The FUNC_PLCISCPU property identifies a PLC box as the central processing unit (the processor) of a PLC controller. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCISCPU property?
output: The FUNC_PLCISCPU property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCISMASTER property identify?
output: The FUNC_PLCISMASTER property identifies a bus port as the master of a bus system / logical network. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCISMASTER property?
output: The FUNC_PLCISMASTER property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCISMOUNTEDONHEADMODULE property identify?
output: The FUNC_PLCISMOUNTEDONHEADMODULE property identifies a PLC card that is placed on a head station or is integrated into it. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCISMOUNTEDONHEADMODULE property?
output: The FUNC_PLCISMOUNTEDONHEADMODULE property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCISPOWERSUPPLY property identify?
output: The FUNC_PLCISPOWERSUPPLY property identifies the device as a power supply. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCISPOWERSUPPLY property?
output: The FUNC_PLCISPOWERSUPPLY property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCMASTER property specify?
output: The FUNC_PLCMASTER property specifies the name of the logical network to which the bus port belongs. This entry has to be unique within a physical network. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCMASTER property?
output: The FUNC_PLCMASTER property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCMASTER_INDIRECT property specify?
output: The FUNC_PLCMASTER_INDIRECT property specifies the name of the logical network of the same single-line bus port at a bus port of the "Overview" representation type. This property is read-only and returns a value of type System.String.

instruction: Is the FUNC_PLCMASTER_INDIRECT property read-only or read-write?
output: The FUNC_PLCMASTER_INDIRECT property is read-only.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCMODULE_ISINTEGRATED property designate?
output: The FUNC_PLCMODULE_ISINTEGRATED property designates the PLC box as an integrated module. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCMODULE_ISINTEGRATED property?
output: The FUNC_PLCMODULE_ISINTEGRATED property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCMODULENAME property represent?
output: The FUNC_PLCMODULENAME property represents the name of a PLC card (identifying for PLC devices during PLC data exchange in the AutomationML AR APC or Studio 5000 Architect format). The property can be displayed in reports and used as a filter criterion in the navigators. Depending on whether the PLC box represents a PLC card, a rack or a CPU, the PLC card name must be unique within a rack, a station or a configuration project. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCMODULENAME property?
output: The FUNC_PLCMODULENAME property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCNO_TARGETSEARCH_FOR_AUTOSYMBOLICADDRESS property do?
output: If the FUNC_PLCNO_TARGETSEARCH_FOR_AUTOSYMBOLICADDRESS property is enabled, no sensor/actuator search is carried out when establishing the "Symbolic address (automatic)" for an I/O connection point. Instead, the address is output when the "Use address if search for symbolic address is unsuccessful" project setting is enabled. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCNO_TARGETSEARCH_FOR_AUTOSYMBOLICADDRESS property?
output: The FUNC_PLCNO_TARGETSEARCH_FOR_AUTOSYMBOLICADDRESS property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCOBJECT_DESCRIPTION property store?
output: The FUNC_PLCOBJECT_DESCRIPTION property stores the PLC object description of a PLC card. It can be automatically entered by the device selection. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCOBJECT_DESCRIPTION property?
output: The FUNC_PLCOBJECT_DESCRIPTION property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the FUNC_PLCOBJECTID property?
output: The FUNC_PLCOBJECTID property is a unique identifier for data exchange / synchronization between Eplan Electric P8 and PLC configuration systems. The object ID uniquely identifies objects of this configuration system. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCOBJECTID property?
output: The FUNC_PLCOBJECTID property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCPAIRCROSSREFERENCE property show?
output: The FUNC_PLCPAIRCROSSREFERENCE property shows cross-references for overview functions. This property is read-only and returns a value of type System.String.

instruction: Is the FUNC_PLCPAIRCROSSREFERENCE property read-only or read-write?
output: The FUNC_PLCPAIRCROSSREFERENCE property is read-only.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCPLUG_AND_CONNPTDESIGNATION property display?
output: The FUNC_PLCPLUG_AND_CONNPTDESIGNATION property displays the combined property from the plug designation and connection point designation of a PLC connection point or device connection point, separated by a colon. At bus ports the bus interface name of the bus port is displayed in addition to the plug designation. This property is read-only and returns a value of type System.String.

instruction: Is the FUNC_PLCPLUG_AND_CONNPTDESIGNATION property read-only or read-write?
output: The FUNC_PLCPLUG_AND_CONNPTDESIGNATION property is read-only.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_AUTOMATIC property provide?
output: The FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_AUTOMATIC property provides the plug designation automatically determined for PLC connection points / channels and device connection points. The plug designation supports the identification of PLC connection points or device connection points. This property is read-only and returns a value of type System.String.

instruction: Is the FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_AUTOMATIC property read-only or read-write?
output: The FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_AUTOMATIC property is read-only.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_MANUAL property store?
output: The FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_MANUAL property stores the designation of the plug connected to the PLC connection point / channel or device connection point. The plug designation can be transferred from the left (or above), as with terminals and supports the identification of PLC connection points or device connection points. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_MANUAL property?
output: The FUNC_PLCPLUG_DESIGNATION_MANUAL property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCPREVENT_NUMERATION property do?
output: The FUNC_PLCPREVENT_NUMERATION property prevents numbering with PLC data from this PLC connection point. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCPREVENT_NUMERATION property?
output: The FUNC_PLCPREVENT_NUMERATION property returns a property value of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCPROCESSDATA property help with?
output: The FUNC_PLCPROCESSDATA property helps you enter and display certain process data, e.g. a measurand or condition information. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCPROCESSDATA property?
output: The FUNC_PLCPROCESSDATA property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCRACK property describe?
output: The FUNC_PLCRACK property describes the rack on which a PLC card is placed. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_PLCRACK property?
output: The FUNC_PLCRACK property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the FUNC_PLCRACK_INDIRECT property output?
output: The FUNC_PLCRACK_INDIRECT property outputs the rack on which the associated PLC card is placed for a PLC connection point. This property is read-only and returns a value of type System.String.

instruction: Is the FUNC_PLCRACK_INDIRECT property read-only or read-write?
output: The FUNC_PLCRACK_INDIRECT property is read-only.

instruction: What is the purpose of the FunctionPropertyList class?
output: The FunctionPropertyList class represents a collection of properties of a Function class. It is used to store and manage property values associated with Function objects.

instruction: What are the base classes of FunctionPropertyList?
output: FunctionPropertyList inherits from FunctionBasePropertyList, which itself inherits from SymbolReferencePropertyList, PlacementPropertyList, and StorableObjectPropertyList, and ultimately from UniversalPropertyList and System.Object.

instruction: How can I access the elements (stored properties) of a FunctionPropertyList?
output: You can access the elements of a FunctionPropertyList using the operator[].

instruction: Explain the difference between online and offline property lists in the context of FunctionPropertyList.
output: If a FunctionPropertyList is 'online', it is associated with the properties of a StorableObject or another property list. Changes made to the online property list are reflected in the related objects. Offline property lists are not directly linked to the objects and changes do not affect the original objects.

instruction: How do I create a persistent (online) FunctionPropertyList in C#?
output: In C#, you can create a persistent FunctionPropertyList using the following code: `FunctionPropertyList oPersistentPropertyList1 = oFunction.Properties;` where `oFunction` is a valid Function object.

instruction: What is the meaning of the FUNC_COMMENT property in FunctionPropertyList?
output: The FUNC_COMMENT property in FunctionPropertyList represents the remark or comment associated with a function. It is identified by the property ID 20045.

instruction: What are some of the properties available in FunctionPropertyList related to cable configuration?
output: Some properties related to cable configuration in FunctionPropertyList include: FUNC_CABLETYPE, FUNC_CABLEWIRECOUNT, FUNC_CABLEWIRECROSSSECTION, FUNC_CABLELENGTH, and FUNC_CABLE_ALLOW_ONLY_MANUEL_CABLE_SELECTION.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Functions3DFilter class?
output: The Functions3DFilter class is used to filter Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Function3D objects when using DMObjectsFinder.

instruction: What is the base class of Functions3DFilter?
output: Functions3DFilter inherits from Placements3DFilter, which in turn inherits from StorableObjectsFilter and AbstractDMObjectFilter.

instruction: How can I filter Functions3D by user-defined properties using Functions3DFilter?
output: You can filter Functions3D by user-defined properties by creating a PagesFilter, defining a MultiLangString for the property value, creating a AnyPropertyId, setting the property value in a PagePropertyList, and then setting the filtered property list in the PagesFilter. Finally, use the DMObjectsFinder to get the filtered pages.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ExactNameMatching property of the FunctionsFilter class?
output: The ExactNameMatching property of the FunctionsFilter class determines whether the filter should match function names exactly. If set to true, only functions with names that precisely match the filter's Name property will be matched. If set to false (the default), functions whose names start with the filter's Name property will be matched.

instruction: How do I set a FunctionsFilter to only include placed functions?
output: Set the IsPlaced property of the FunctionsFilter to true.

instruction: What is the purpose of the SetIdentifyingPropertyList method in the FunctionsFilter class?
output: The SetIdentifyingPropertyList method in the FunctionsFilter class sets the FunctionBasePropertyList or FunctionPropertyList that identifies the matching Functions. This means that only Functions with properties matching the provided list will be considered as matching the filter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GetAllFunctionsWithSameName method in the FunctionVerification class?
output: The GetAllFunctionsWithSameName method in the FunctionVerification class retrieves all functions within the project that share the same name as a specified function. This method is designed to be called within the Execute function of a custom verification.

instruction: What are the parameters of the Cables method that takes a Project object?
output: The Cables method that takes a Project object has the following parameters: oProject (Project), strCreateSchemaName (string), strNumberingSchemaName (string), strAutoSelSchemaName (string), bRegenrateConnections (bool), bKeepOldNames (bool), nStartValue (int), nStepValue (int), and bOnlyAutomaticCables (bool).

instruction: What happens if you pass an empty string to a scheme parameter in the Cables method?
output: If you pass an empty string to a scheme parameter in the Cables method, the last used scheme will be used which is currently set in the GUI. If no scheme exists under the given scheme name, an exception will be thrown.

instruction: What are the parameters of the Connections method that takes a Project object and a boolean?
output: The Connections method that takes a Project object and a boolean has the following parameters: oProject (Project) and bRebuildAllConnections (bool).

instruction: What does the bRebuildAllConnections parameter do in the Connections method?
output: If bRebuildAllConnections is set to true, all connections in the project are rebuilt. If it is set to false, only connections are updated.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GraphicalLayer class?
output: The GraphicalLayer class represents a graphical layer of the project and is used to modify layer properties.

instruction: How do you create a new GraphicalLayer object detached from the project?
output: You can create a new GraphicalLayer object detached from the project using the default constructor: `GraphicalLayer()`.  This object needs to be added to a GraphicalLayerTable to be stored.

instruction: What happens if you call the Store() method on a GraphicalLayer object?
output: The Store() method stores the modified layer's properties into the project. However, if the object is not connected with any GraphicalLayerTable, the method will return false.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method of the GraphicalLayer class?
output: The Create method creates a new graphical layer within the graphical table. It calls GraphicalLayerTable.AddLayer() internally and merges the returned object with the created object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the SetVisibilityDependingOnLayer method in the GraphicalPlacement class?
output: The SetVisibilityDependingOnLayer method sets the visibility of the graphical instance to depend on the layer setting.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GraphicalPlacementPropertyList class?
output: The GraphicalPlacementPropertyList class represents a collection of properties of a GraphicalPlacement class.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GridLineMate class?
output: The GridLineMate class represents a grid line mate object that forms a grid mate.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GridMate property in the GridLineMate class?
output: The GridMate property in the GridLineMate class returns the grid mate.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Group constructor?
output: The Group constructor creates a new Group object. However, it throws a NotImplementedException and should never be used. It was designed only for COM compatibility.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method in the Group class?
output: The Create method creates a new Group object and places it on the page of the first placement given as a parameter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DeepUnGroup method in the Group class?
output: The DeepUnGroup method removes all Placements from a group and its subgroups. The method has two overloads, one that removes subgroups and one that doesn't.

instruction: What is the purpose of the IsEditable method in the Group class?
output: The IsEditable method checks if the Group is editable.

instruction: What is the purpose of the RemoveSubPlacement method in the Group class?
output: The RemoveSubPlacement method removes a Placement from the group and from the project. The Placement object passed into this method is no longer valid.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GraphicalLayerTable class?
output: The GraphicalLayerTable class represents the table of all graphical layers of the project. It is used for creating, deleting, merging, and retrieving project's layers.

instruction: How do you retrieve a specific layer from the GraphicalLayerTable?
output: You can retrieve a specific layer from the GraphicalLayerTable using the Layer property, either by its ID (Layer(Int16)) or by its name (Layer(String)).

instruction: What happens if you try to remove a layer that still contains graphical objects?
output: If you try to remove a layer that still contains graphical objects, the RemoveLayer method will throw an exception of type Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.DataModelException.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Group` class in the EPLAN API?
output: The `Group` class in the EPLAN API represents grouped Placements. It allows you to organize and manipulate multiple placements as a single unit.

instruction: What are the base classes of the `Group` class?
output: The `Group` class inherits from `Placement`, which in turn inherits from `StorableObject`.  Therefore, the base classes are `System.Object`, `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.StorableObject`, and `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Placement`.

instruction: What methods does the `Group` class have?
output: The `Group` class has the following public methods: `CopyTo`, `Create`, `DeepUnGroup`, `Dispose()`, `Equals`, `GetBoundingBox`, `GetHashCode`, `GetTypeName`, `InsertSubPlacement`, `InsertSubPlacements`, `IsEditable`, `IsGroupValid`, `LockObject`, `Move`, `Remove`, `RemoveSubPlacement`, `RemoveSubPlacements`, `Scale`, `SmartLock`, `ToStringIdentifier`, and `UnGroup`.

instruction: How do I create a new `Group` object using the EPLAN API?
output: You can create a new `Group` object using the `Create` method. This method is overloaded. One version creates a new Group object and places it on the page of the first placement given as a parameter.  Another version creates a new Group3D object.

instruction: What is the difference between `UnGroup` and `DeepUnGroup` methods of the `Group` class?
output: The `UnGroup` method removes Placements only from a group. The `DeepUnGroup` method removes all Placements from a group and its sub-groups.  If the boolean parameter is set to true, `DeepUnGroup` will also remove SubGroups.

instruction: What properties can I access using the `Group` class in the EPLAN API?
output: The `Group` class has the following public properties: `CrossReferencedObjectsAll`, `DatabaseIdentifier`, `DrawingOrder`, `Group`, `IsLocked`, `IsPlaced`, `IsReadOnly`, `IsSetAsVisible`, `IsTransient`, `IsValid`, `Location`, `ObjectIdentifier`, `Page`, `Project`, `Properties`, `SubPlacements`, `Tooltip`, and `TypeIdentifier`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Location` property of the `Group` class?
output: The `Location` property of the `Group` class gets or sets the placement's location. It is an overridden property.

instruction: What is the `Properties` property of the `Group` class used for?
output: The `Properties` property of the `Group` class is a .NET Property that enables access to P8 properties of the Group object.

instruction: What does the `SubPlacements` property of the `Group` class return?
output: The `SubPlacements` property of the `Group` class returns all grouped Placements.

instruction: What is the data type of the `Location` property of the `Group` class?
output: The `Location` property of the `Group` class is of type `PointD`.

instruction: What exception is thrown when trying to access the `Location` property of a `SymbolVariant` object?
output: The `ForbiddenOperationException` is thrown when trying to access the `Location` property of a `SymbolVariant` object.

instruction: What does the `Properties` property of the `Group` class provide access to?
output: The `Properties` property of the `Group` class provides access to the P8 properties of the Group object.

instruction: What is the data type of the `Properties` property of the `Group` class?
output: The `Properties` property of the `Group` class is of type `GroupPropertyList`.

instruction: What is the data type of the `SubPlacements` property of the `Group` class?
output: The `SubPlacements` property of the `Group` class is of type `Placement[]`.

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown when accessing the `SubPlacements` property?
output: The `Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException` and `ForbiddenOperationException` exceptions can be thrown when accessing the `SubPlacements` property.

instruction: What are the public properties available for the `Group` class?
output: The `Group` class has the following public properties: `CrossReferencedObjectsAll`, `DatabaseIdentifier`, `DrawingOrder`, `Group`, `IsLocked`, `IsPlaced`, `IsReadOnly`, `IsSetAsVisible`, `IsTransient`, `IsValid`, `Location`, `ObjectIdentifier`, `Page`, `Project`, `Properties`, `SubPlacements`, `Tooltip`, and `TypeIdentifier`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Group3D` class represents grouped Placement3Ds.

instruction: What is the base class of the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Group3D` class inherits from `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.StorableObject`.

instruction: What are the public constructors of the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Group3D` class has one public constructor: `Group3D()`, which is the default constructor and creates a `Group3D` object.

instruction: What methods are available in the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Group3D` class has the following public methods: `AddMate`, `Create`, `Dispose()`, `Equals`, `GetHashCode`, `GetMates`, `GetTypeName`, `InsertSubPlacement3D`, `InsertSubPlacement3Ds`, `LockObject`, `Move`, `Remove`, `RemoveSubPlacement3D`, `RemoveSubPlacement3Ds`, `SmartLock`, `ToStringIdentifier`, and `UnGroup`.

instruction: How do I create a new `Group3D` object using the API?
output: You can create a new `Group3D` object using the default constructor `Group3D()`.  You can also use the `Create` method to create a new `Group3D` object, which takes an array of `Placement3D` objects as a parameter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `AddMate(Mate)` method in the `Group3D` class?
output: The `AddMate(Mate)` method adds a mate to a group. The source object is taken from a custom mate (if exists), otherwise it is the first subplacement of a group.

instruction: What are the parameters of the `AddMate(Mate, Placement3D)` method?
output: The `AddMate(Mate, Placement3D)` method takes two parameters: `customMate` (a `Mate` object) and `placement3D` (a `Placement3D` object).

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Create` method in the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Create` method creates a new `Group3D` object.

instruction: What are the parameters of the `Create` method in the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Create` method in the `Group3D` class takes one parameter: `placement3Ds`, which is an array of `Placement3D` objects.

instruction: What exception is thrown by the `Create` method if the number of 3D placements is less than two?
output: The `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.InsufficientElementsCountException` is thrown when the number of 3D placements is less than two.

instruction: What does the `GetMates` method of the `Group3D` class do?
output: The `GetMates` method gets group mates.

instruction: What does the `InsertSubPlacement3D` method do?
output: The `InsertSubPlacement3D` method inserts a `Placement3D` to the 3D group.

instruction: What is the return value of the `InsertSubPlacement3D` method?
output: The `InsertSubPlacement3D` method returns a boolean value: `true` if successful.

instruction: What does the `InsertSubPlacement3Ds` method do?
output: The `InsertSubPlacement3Ds` method inserts `Placement3D`s to the 3D group.

instruction: What is the return value of the `InsertSubPlacement3Ds` method?
output: The `InsertSubPlacement3Ds` method returns a boolean value: `true` if successful.

instruction: What does the `Move` method of the `Group3D` class do?
output: The `Move` method moves the `Group3D` object relative to the absolute axis origin.

instruction: What are the parameters of the `Move` method in the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Move` method takes three parameters: `x`, `y`, and `z`, all of type `double`. These represent the move values along the X, Y, and Z axes, respectively.

instruction: What does the `Remove` method of the `Group3D` class do?
output: The `Remove` method removes the 3D group and all grouped `Placement3D`s.

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the `Remove` method?
output: The `Remove` method can throw `Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException` and `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.RemoveFailedException`.

instruction: What does the `RemoveSubPlacement3D(Placement3D)` method do?
output: The `RemoveSubPlacement3D(Placement3D)` method removes a `Placement3D` from the 3D group and from the project. The `Placement3D` object passed into this method is no longer valid.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `removeEmptyGroup3D` parameter in the `RemoveSubPlacement3D(Placement3D, Boolean)` method?
output: The `removeEmptyGroup3D` parameter in the `RemoveSubPlacement3D(Placement3D, Boolean)` method, when set to `true`, will remove the `Group3D` object if it becomes empty after removing the specified `Placement3D`.

instruction: What does the `RemoveSubPlacement3Ds` method do?
output: The `RemoveSubPlacement3Ds` method removes `Placement3D`s from the 3D group and from the project. The `Placement3D` objects passed into this method are no longer valid.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `removeEmptyGroup3D` parameter in the `RemoveSubPlacement3Ds(Placement3D[], Boolean)` method?
output: The `removeEmptyGroup3D` parameter in the `RemoveSubPlacement3Ds(Placement3D[], Boolean)` method, when set to `true`, will remove the `Group3D` object when it becomes empty after removing the specified `Placement3D`s.

instruction: What does the `UnGroup()` method do?
output: The `UnGroup()` method removes all `Placement3D`s from the 3D group. The `Group3D` object itself will not be removed.

instruction: What does the `UnGroup(Placement3D)` method do?
output: The `UnGroup(Placement3D)` method removes a `Placement3D` only from the 3D group.

instruction: What are the parameters of the `UnGroup(Placement3D, Boolean, Boolean)` method?
output: The `UnGroup(Placement3D, Boolean, Boolean)` method takes three parameters: `placement3D` (the `Placement3D` to remove), `removeEmptyGroup3D` (a boolean indicating whether to remove the `Group3D` if it becomes empty), and `redraw` (a boolean indicating whether to redraw the GED after ungrouping).

instruction: What are the public properties of the `Group3D` class?
output: The `Group3D` class has the following public properties: `CrossReferencedObjectsAll`, `DatabaseIdentifier`, `GroupHandleMate`, `IsLocked`, `IsPlaced`, `IsReadOnly`, `IsTransient`, `IsValid`, `ObjectIdentifier`, `Parent`, `Project`, `Properties`, `SubPlacement3Ds`, and `TypeIdentifier`.

instruction: What does the `GroupHandleMate` property of the `Group3D` class return?
output: The `GroupHandleMate` property gets the handle mate.

instruction: What does the `IsPlaced` property of the `Group3D` class return?
output: The `IsPlaced` property returns `true` if the `Group3D` object is placed.

instruction: What does the `Parent` property of the `Group3D` class return?
output: The `Parent` property gets the parent of the `Group3D` object.

instruction: What does the `Properties` property of the `Group3D` class provide access to?
output: The `Properties` property of the `Group3D` class provides access to the P8 properties of the 3D group.

instruction: What does the `SubPlacement3Ds` property of the `Group3D` class return?
output: The `SubPlacement3Ds` property returns all grouped `Placement3D`s.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Group3DPropertyList` class represents a collection of properties of the `Group3D` class.

instruction: What is the base class of the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Group3DPropertyList` class inherits from `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.StorableObjectPropertyList`.

instruction: How are the elements of the `Group3DPropertyList` accessed?
output: The elements of the `Group3DPropertyList` are accessed using the `Property` property, which is an overloaded method that uses operator[] to access its elements (stored properties).

instruction: What are the public constructors of the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Group3DPropertyList` class has two public constructors: a default constructor `Group3DPropertyList()` and a constructor `Group3DPropertyList(Group3D obj)` which should be used internally only.

instruction: What are the public properties of the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Group3DPropertyList` class has the following public properties: `ExistingIds`, `ExistingValues`, `Parent`, `Property`, and `PROPUSER_DBOBJECTID`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Property(AnyPropertyId)` property in the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Property(AnyPropertyId)` property is a method used by operator[] in order to access properties by `AnyPropertyId`.

instruction: What is the return type of the `Property(AnyPropertyId)` property in the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Property(AnyPropertyId)` property returns a `PropertyValue` object.

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the `Property(AnyPropertyId)` property?
output: The `Property(AnyPropertyId)` property can throw `Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException` and `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.PropertyNotFoundException`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32)` property in the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32)` property is a method used by operator[] in order to access indexed properties by `AnyPropertyId`.

instruction: What is the return type of the `Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32)` property in the `Group3DPropertyList` class?
output: The `Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32)` property returns a `PropertyValue` object.

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the `Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32)` property?
output: The `Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32)` property can throw `Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException`, `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.PropertyNotFoundException`, `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.InvalidIndexException`, and `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.SettingValueFailedException`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `HandleMate` class represents a handle mate.

instruction: What is the base class of the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `HandleMate` class inherits from `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.PointMate`, which in turn inherits from `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Mate`.

instruction: What are the public constructors of the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `HandleMate` class has one public constructor: `HandleMate()`, which is the default constructor.

instruction: What are the public properties of the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `HandleMate` class has the following public properties: `Description`, `Group`, `IsBaseMate`, `IsReadOnly`, `IsUserDefined`, `MatchingMateNames`, `Name`, `Placement`, `Rotation`, `RotationType`, `Source`, `Transformation`.

instruction: What methods are available in the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `HandleMate` class has the following public methods: `CalculateSnapTransformation`, `CanSnapTo`, `Create`, `Dispose`, `Equals`, `Remove`, `SnapTo`, `StoreToObject`, and `Transform`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Group` property of the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `Group` property retrieves the group that a handle belongs to.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Create(Mate)` method in the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `Create(Mate)` method creates a handle mate from an existing mate.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Create(MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method in the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `Create(MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method creates a handle point with all mounting points permitted.

instruction: What are the parameters of the `Create(MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method in the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `Create(MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method takes two parameters: `pMLSDescription` (a `MultiLangString` object) and `oPosition` (a `Matrix3D` object).

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the `Create(MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method?
output: The `Create(MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method can throw `System.ArgumentNullException` and `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectAlreadyCreatedException`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `Create(String[], MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method in the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `Create(String[], MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method creates a handle with a list of assigned mounting points.

instruction: What are the parameters of the `Create(String[], MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method in the `HandleMate` class?
output: The `Create(String[], MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method takes three parameters: `matchingMateNames` (an array of strings), `pMLSDescription` (a `MultiLangString` object), and `matrix` (a `Matrix3D` object).

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the `Create(String[], MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method?
output: The `Create(String[], MultiLangString, Matrix3D)` method can throw `System.ArgumentNullException` and `Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectAlreadyCreatedException`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `FunctionCategory` property in the `Harness` class?

output: The `Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException` can be thrown when the category cannot be retrieved or set.

instruction: What does the `Objects` property of the `Harness` class return?
output: The `Objects` property returns an array of `StorableObject`s which contains the same harness name in property `HARNESS_NAME`.

instruction: What does the `WireHarnessName` property of the `Harness` class do?
output: The `WireHarnessName` property gets or sets the wire harness name.

instruction: What is the data type of the `WireHarnessName` property in the `Harness` class?
output: The `WireHarnessName` property is of type `string`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the `HarnessService` class?
output: The `HarnessService` class provides harness functionalities such as import or export.

instruction: What are the public methods of the `HarnessService` class?
output: The `HarnessService` class has the following public methods: `Dispose`, `ExportData`, and `ImportData`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Image class in the EPLAN API?
output: The Image class represents an image within the EPLAN data model.

instruction: What is the base class for the Image class?
output: The Image class inherits from the Rectangle class.

instruction: What are the public properties available in the Image class?
output: The public properties of the Image class include Angle, ChamferRadius, CrossReferencedObjectsAll, DatabaseIdentifier, DrawingOrder, FileName, FillPattern, Group, HasChamfers, Height, IsLocked, IsPlaced, IsReadOnly, IsSetAsVisible, IsSurfaceFilled, IsTransient, IsValid, IsVisible, Layer, LayerId, Location, LowerLeftCorner, ObjectIdentifier, Page, Pen, PhysicalDimension, Project, Properties, Tooltip, TypeIdentifier, UpperRightCorner, Width, and WidthToHeighRatioFix.

instruction: What is the purpose of the FileName property of the Image class?
output: The FileName property of the Image class represents the full file name of the image file.

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown when setting the FileName property?
output: The System.ArgumentNullException is thrown when strName is null. The Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException is thrown when the file name cannot be set.

instruction: What does the FillPattern property of the Image class do?
output: The FillPattern property gets or sets the fill pattern of the image. Note that Pattern types Bitmap, Eplan and System are not supported.

instruction: What is the meaning of the IsSurfaceFilled property in the Image class?
output: The IsSurfaceFilled property specifies whether the rectangle representing the image is filled. If true, the rectangle is filled; if false, it is not.

instruction: What does the PhysicalDimension property of the Image class return?
output: The PhysicalDimension property returns the size of the bitmap in points, as a PointD object containing the height and width.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Properties property in the Image class?
output: The Properties property provides access to the P8 properties of the Image object.

instruction: What does the WidthToHeighRatioFix property indicate?
output: The WidthToHeighRatioFix property is a boolean value that indicates whether the aspect ratio of the image is the same as in the original image file. If true, the aspect ratio is fixed.

instruction: What methods are available for the Image class?
output: The Image class has the following public methods: CopyTo, Create, CreateTransient, Dispose(), Equals, GetBoundingBox, GetHashCode, GetPhysicalDimensionOfImage, GetTypeName, LockObject, Remove, Scale, SetArea, SetAreaByHeigth, SetAreaByWidth, SetFile, SetVisibilityDependingOnLayer, SmartLock, ToStringIdentifier.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ImageCopyException?
output: The ImageCopyException is thrown when an image cannot be copied to a new destination.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESCRIPTION_ML property in ImagePropertyList?
output: The DESCRIPTION_ML property in ImagePropertyList represents the description of the image file, allowing for multilingual entries.

instruction: What is the data type of the DESCRIPTION_ML property?
output: The DESCRIPTION_ML property returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What does the FILE property in ImagePropertyList represent?
output: The FILE property in ImagePropertyList shows the image file or the document with the hyperlink.

instruction: What is the data type of the FILE property?
output: The FILE property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: How can you access properties of an Image object using the ImagePropertyList?
output: You can access properties using the Property property, which is overloaded.  You can access properties by AnyPropertyId, by Properties.Image id, or by index.

instruction: What are the available methods for the ImagePropertyList class?
output: The ImagePropertyList class has the following public methods: CopyTo, Dispose(), Exists, and getPropList.

instruction: What are the predefined classifications for MDParts Database messages?
output: The predefined classifications for MDParts Database messages are Error, Hint, Unknown, and Warning.

instruction: What are the possible values for IMessage.Region?
output: The possible values for IMessage.Region are: BlackBoxes, Cables, Connections, CrossReferences, Devices, DeviceTags, DrillingPattern, Eplan21Imports, Eplan5Imports, Externals, Fluid, ForeignLanguages, Harness, InterruptionPoints, Macro, MountingLayout3D, Others, PanelLayout2D, PartMasterData, PartsData, PlaceholderObject, PLCBus, Plugs, PrePlanning, PrePlanningDepth, ProjectCompare, ProjectSettings, Reports, Subprojects, Terminals, Topology, and Unknown.

instruction: What are the possible values for IMessage.Classification?
output: The possible values for IMessage.Classification are Error, Hint, Unknown, and Warning.

instruction: What does the DoHelp method of the IMessage interface do?
output: The DoHelp method is called by EPLAN when the help text for the message should be shown. The method itself must call the appropriate help system with the correct language, typically by calling a dialog or message box.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GetMessageText method in the IMessage interface?
output: The GetMessageText method is called by EPLAN when the message text should be shown. The method is responsible for retrieving the text in the correct language, considering potential placeholders for substitution.

instruction: What is the purpose of the OnRegister method in the IMessage interface?
output: The OnRegister method is called by EPLAN when a new project message is added to the system. If a new project message was added to a registered add-in, the add-in must be registered again.

instruction: What are the predefined regions for project messages?
output: The predefined regions for project messages are: BlackBoxes, Cables, Connections, CrossReferences, Devices, DeviceTags, DrillingPattern, Eplan21Imports, Eplan5Imports, Externals, Fluid, ForeignLanguages, Harness, InterruptionPoints, Macro, MountingLayout3D, Others, PanelLayout2D, PartMasterData, PartsData, PlaceholderObject, PLCBus, Plugs, PrePlanning, PrePlanningDepth, ProjectCompare, ProjectSettings, Reports, Subprojects, Terminals, Topology, and Unknown.

instruction: What are the possible values for IMessage.ProjectMessageCreationType?
output: The possible values for IMessage.ProjectMessageCreationType are: Explicit, Offline, Online, and Undefined.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DXFDWGFiles method in the Import class?
output: The DXFDWGFiles method creates an EPLAN macro from a DXF / DWG file.

instruction: What are the parameters for the DXFDWGFiles(Project, string, string, string) method?
output: The parameters for the DXFDWGFiles(Project, string, string, string) method are: oProject (Project), strDXFMacroDir (string), strDestinationDir (string), and strScheme (string).

instruction: What are the parameters for the DXFDWGFiles(string, string, string, string) method?
output: The parameters for the DXFDWGFiles(string, string, string, string) method are: strFullLinkFileName (string), strDXFMacroDir (string), strDestinationDir (string), and strScheme (string).

instruction: What is the purpose of the DXFPage method?
output: The DXFPage method imports a DXF or DWG file into an existing page.

instruction: What are the parameters for the DXFPage(string, Page, Transformation, string) method?
output: The parameters for the DXFPage(string, Page, Transformation, string) method are: strDXFFileName (string), oPage (Page), oTransformation (Transformation), and strScheme (string).

instruction: What are the parameters for the DXFPage(string, Page, Transformation, string, ArrayList) method?
output: The parameters for the DXFPage(string, Page, Transformation, string, ArrayList) method are: strDXFFileName (string), oPage (Page), oTransformation (Transformation), strScheme (string), and importedPlacements (ArrayList).

instruction: What are the parameters for the DXFPage(string, string, string, Transformation, string) method?
output: The parameters for the DXFPage(string, string, string, Transformation, string) method are: strDXFFileName (string), strFullLinkFileName (string), strPageName (string), oTransformation (Transformation), and strScheme (string).

instruction: What are the parameters for the DXFPage(string, string, string, Transformation, string, ArrayList) method?
output: The parameters for the DXFPage(string, string, string, Transformation, string, ArrayList) method are: strDXFFileName (string), strFullLinkFileName (string), strPageName (string), oTransformation (Transformation), strScheme (string), and importedPlacements (ArrayList).

instruction: What is the purpose of the PDFComments method?
output: The PDFComments method imports comments from a PDF file.

instruction: What are the parameters for the PDFComments(Project, string) method?
output: The parameters for the PDFComments(Project, string) method are: oProject (Project) and strFileName (string).

instruction: What are the parameters for the PDFComments(string, string) method?
output: The parameters for the PDFComments(string, string) method are: strFullLinkFileName (string) and strFileName (string).

instruction: What is the purpose of the PXFProject method?
output: The PXFProject method creates a project from an imported PXF file.

instruction: What are the parameters for the PXFProject(string, string) method?
output: The parameters for the PXFProject(string, string) method are: strPXFFilePath (string) and strDestinationPath (string).

instruction: What is the purpose of the ArePlanningObjectsInserted property in the Insert class?
output: The ArePlanningObjectsInserted property indicates whether additional planning objects are inserted from a macro into the project.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ArePlanningObjectsStructureMerged property in the Insert class?
output: The ArePlanningObjectsStructureMerged property indicates whether the planning objects structure will be merged with existing nodes or renumbered and added.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PlanningSegment property in the Insert class?
output: The PlanningSegment property determines the planning segment to which all functions inserted from a window macro on a page will be assigned.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DesignSpace method in the Import3D class?
output: The DesignSpace method imports a Step file as a design space.

instruction: What is the purpose of the JT method in the Import3D class?
output: The JT method imports a 3D file.

instruction: What are the parameters for the JT method?
output: The parameters for the JT method are: oProject (Project) and strFileName (string).

instruction: What is the return value of the JT method?
output: The JT method returns the InstallationSpace to which the JT file was imported.

instruction: What is the purpose of the STEP method in the Import3D class?
output: The STEP method imports a STEP file.

instruction: What are the parameters for the STEP method?
output: The parameters for the STEP method are: oProject (Project) and strFileName (string).

instruction: What is the return value of the STEP method?
output: The STEP method returns the InstallationSpace to which the step file was imported.

instruction: What does the IsInsertInteraction property indicate?
output: The IsInsertInteraction property returns `true` if the interaction is used to insert one or more placements on a page.

instruction: What does the InsertedItems property do?
output: The InsertedItems property returns placements inserted by the interaction.

instruction: What does the IncorrectObjectTypeException indicate?
output: The IncorrectObjectTypeException is thrown when the create function is invoked with the wrong object.

instruction: What are the possible values for the Insert.MoveKind enumeration?
output: The possible values for the Insert.MoveKind enumeration are: Absolute and Relative.

instruction: What does the SymbolMacro method do?
output: The SymbolMacro method places a symbol macro onto a given position of a page.

instruction: What are the parameters for the SymbolMacro(string, int, Page, PointD, Insert.MoveKind) method?
output: The parameters for the SymbolMacro(string, int, Page, PointD, Insert.MoveKind) method are: strEMSFileName (string), nVariant (int), oPage (Page), oPlacement (PointD), and moveCondition (Insert.MoveKind).

instruction: What does the WindowMacro method do?
output: The WindowMacro method places a window macro onto a given position of a page.

instruction: What are the parameters for the WindowMacro(string, int, Page, PointD, Insert.MoveKind) method?
output: The parameters for the WindowMacro(string, int, Page, PointD, Insert.MoveKind) method are: strEMAFileName (string), nVariant (int), oPage (Page), oPlacement (PointD), and moveCondition (Insert.MoveKind).

instruction: What does the PageMacro method do?
output: The PageMacro method inserts a page macro into a project.

instruction: What are the parameters for the PageMacro(string, Page, Project, bool) method?
output: The parameters for the PageMacro(string, Page, Project, bool) method are: strEMPFileName (string), oInsertAfterPage (Page), oProject (Project), and overwrite (bool).

instruction: What does the PrePlanningMacro method do?
output: The PrePlanningMacro method inserts the pre-planning macros below the given structure segment.

instruction: What are the parameters for the PrePlanningMacro method?
output: The parameters for the PrePlanningMacro method are: strMacroPath (string), pProject (Project), and pParent (PlanningSegment).

instruction: What does the DesignSpace method do?
output: The DesignSpace method imports a Step file as a design space.

instruction: What are the parameters for the DesignSpace(Project, String, String) method?
output: The parameters for the DesignSpace(Project, String, String) method are: oProject (Project), strFileName (string), and strScheme (string).

instruction: What is the purpose of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_DESIGNATION property?
output: The INSTALLATIONSPACE_DESIGNATION property represents the layout space name.

instruction: What is the purpose of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_GRIDSIZE property?
output: The INSTALLATIONSPACE_GRIDSIZE property defines the grid size for graphical editing in the 3D view.

instruction: What is the purpose of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACRO_DESCRIPTION property?
output: The INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACRO_DESCRIPTION property provides the description text for the 3D macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACRO_NAME property?
output: The INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACRO_NAME property represents the file name of the 3D macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACRO_VERSION property?
output: The INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACRO_VERSION property represents the version designation of the macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACROSOURCEPROJECT property?
output: The INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACROSOURCEPROJECT property represents the project name of the project used to create the macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACROSOURCEREF property?
output: The INSTALLATIONSPACE_MACROSOURCEREF property represents the macro source or reference.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_MACRO_DESCRIPTION property?
output: The PAGE_MACRO_DESCRIPTION property provides the description text for the macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_MACRO_NAME property?
output: The PAGE_MACRO_NAME property represents the file name of the macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_CHANGE property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_CHANGE property represents the reason for a revision change.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_DATE property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_DATE property represents the date on which a change was carried out within a revision.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_DESCRIPTION property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_DESCRIPTION property represents the revision description.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_EDITINGAREA property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_EDITINGAREA property represents the defined working section from change tracking.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_NAME property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_NAME property represents the revision index.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_PROJ_REV property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_PROJ_REV property represents the associated project revision.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_TIME property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_TIME property represents the revision time.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_REVISION_LOG_USER property?
output: The PAGE_REVISION_LOG_USER property represents the user who created the revision.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USERCODE property?
output: The PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USERCODE property shows the user code that was specified in the user settings.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USEREMAIL property?
output: The PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USEREMAIL property shows the e-mail address that was specified in the user settings.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USERNAME property?
output: The PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USERNAME property shows the user name that was specified in the user settings.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USERPHONE property?
output: The PROJ_REVISION_LOG_USERPHONE property shows the phone number that was specified in the user settings.

instruction: What is the difference between FUNC_DEVICETAG_FULL and FUNC_DEVICETAG_MAIN properties?
output: FUNC_DEVICETAG_FULL returns the full device tag without project structures, while FUNC_DEVICETAG_MAIN returns the superior device tag without project structures.

instruction: How can I programmatically access the value of the INSTALLATIONSPACE_DESIGNATION property?
output: You can access the INSTALLATIONSPACE_DESIGNATION property using the property ID 36452.

instruction: What is the meaning of the property FUNCTION3D_OPENING_DIAMETER?
output: The FUNCTION3D_OPENING_DIAMETER property represents the diameter of the hole for a cut-out, identified by the property ID 36006.

instruction: What is the purpose of the InstallationSpacePropertyList class?
output: The InstallationSpacePropertyList class represents a collection of properties of the InstallationSpace class.

instruction: What are the base classes of InstallationSpacePropertyList?
output: The InstallationSpacePropertyList class inherits from Function3DPropertyList, which in turn inherits from Placement3DPropertyList, StorableObjectPropertyList, and UniversalPropertyList, and ultimately System.Object.

instruction: How can I access the elements (stored properties) of an InstallationSpacePropertyList?
output: The elements of an InstallationSpacePropertyList are accessed using the operator[].

instruction: How do I create a persistent property list in C#?
output: In C#, you can create a persistent property list like this: `FunctionPropertyList oPersistentPropertyList1 = oFunction.Properties; oPersistentPropertyList1.FUNC_COMMENT = "Comment";`

instruction: How do I create a transient property list in C#?
output: In C#, you can create a transient property list like this: `FunctionPropertyList oTransientPropertyList = new FunctionPropertyList(); oTransientPropertyList.FUNC_COMMENT = "Test comment"; oFunction.Properties.FUNC_COMMENT = oTransientPropertyList.FUNC_COMMENT; oTransientPropertyList.FUNC_COMMENT = "Transient comment";`

instruction: What is the meaning of the DefaultMember attribute on the InstallationSpacePropertyList class?
output: The DefaultMember attribute on the InstallationSpacePropertyList class indicates that the 'Property' member is the default member, which is used by the operator[].

instruction: What are some of the public properties available in the InstallationSpacePropertyList class?
output: Some of the public properties include ARTICLE_MOUNTINGSITE, AUTOMATIONML_OBJECTID, CABLING_DUCT_TYPES, DESIGNATION_FULLFUNCTIONALASSIGNMENT, DESIGNATION_FULLINSTALLATIONNUMBER, DESIGNATION_FULLLOCATION, and many more. These properties are inherited from base classes.

instruction: What does the InsufficientElementsCountException indicate?
output: The InsufficientElementsCountException is thrown when a group cannot be created because there are not enough elements.

instruction: What is the base class for InsufficientElementsCountException?
output: The InsufficientElementsCountException class inherits from InvalidArgumentException, which in turn inherits from SystemException, Exception, and System.Object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the InsufficientLicenceException?
output: The InsufficientLicenceException is thrown when the number of logical pages in the project is equal to or greater than the maximum number of logical pages allowed by the license, and the user attempts to add another logical page.

instruction: What is the base class for InsufficientLicenceException?
output: The InsufficientLicenceException class inherits from DataModelException, which in turn inherits from BaseException, ApplicationException, Exception, and System.Object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Interaction class?
output: The Interaction class is the base class for special GED (Graphical Editor) interactions in EPLAN.

instruction: What is the meaning of the OnStart method in the Interaction class?
output: The OnStart method is called after the start of an interaction.

instruction: What is the meaning of the OnSuccess method in the Interaction class?
output: The OnSuccess method is called after successful input of all necessary data as a reaction to the request code Success.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Description property in the Interaction class?
output: The Description property sets the description of the undo step for the interaction. This text is used as the description of the undo step which is being created to handle changes made in method OnSuccess.

instruction: When is the OnCancel method called?
output: The OnCancel method is called after the abort of an interaction. This occurs, for example, in case of switching a page or pressing the 'Escape' key.

instruction: What is the purpose of the OnPoint method?
output: The OnPoint method is called after a point is input by the user via mouse or keyboard. This means the user has clicked into the CAD window or opened the command line and input the coordinates of a point.

instruction: What is the purpose of the OnDrawCursor method?
output: The OnDrawCursor method is called to get drawable objects for the cursor representation.

instruction: What is the purpose of the OnSelect method?
output: The OnSelect method is called after object selection in the graphical editor by the user.

instruction: What is the purpose of the SetSelection method?
output: The SetSelection method changes the selection. It should only be used inside the OnSelect() method.

instruction: What is the purpose of the IsReadOnly property?
output: The IsReadOnly property returns `true` if the function does not change the project database.

instruction: What is the purpose of the IsInsertInteraction property?
output: The IsInsertInteraction property returns `true` if the interaction is used to insert one or more placements on a page.

instruction: What is the purpose of the InteractionAttribute class?
output: The InteractionAttribute class is used to define attributes for GED interactions.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Name property in the InteractionAttribute class?
output: The Name property specifies the name under which the interaction will be registered.

instruction: What does the Ordinal property in InteractionAttribute control?
output: The Ordinal property controls the overload level of the interaction. A new interaction can overwrite an existing interaction with the same Name if the ordinal number of the new interaction is higher.

instruction: What does the Prio property in InteractionAttribute control?
output: The Prio property sets the priority of the interaction. If an interaction is started while another interaction is running, the one with the lower priority will be deactivated until all interactions with higher priority are finished.

instruction: What is the purpose of the InteractionContext class?
output: The InteractionContext class is used to pass context-specific information to and from an interaction.

instruction: What does the GetResult method do?
output: The GetResult method gets the Block of Context Parameters of the InteractionContext.

instruction: What does the SetContextParameter method do?
output: The SetContextParameter method sets a block of context parameters (as a ContextParameterBlock object).

instruction: What methods does the InteractionContext class inherit from the Eplan.EplApi.Base.Context class?
output: The InteractionContext class inherits the following methods from the Eplan.EplApi.Base.Context class: AddParameter, Dispose(), GetEContext, GetParameter, GetParameterCount, GetParameters, and GetStrings.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Placements property in the InteractionContext class?
output: The Placements property in the InteractionContext class represents the placements in the current context.

instruction: What is the return type of the Placements property?
output: The Placements property has the type `Placement[]` in C# and `array<Placement^>^` in C++.

instruction: What are the public properties of the InteractionContext class?
output: The InteractionContext class has one public property: Placements.

instruction: What is the purpose of the InteractionEvent class?
output: The InteractionEvent class is used to pass information about an event that has occurred.

instruction: What is the purpose of the InteractionEvent constructor that takes an InteractionEvent as a parameter?
output: The InteractionEvent(InteractionEvent event) constructor is a copy constructor.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Dispose method in the InteractionEvent class?
output: The Dispose method in the InteractionEvent class is a destructor for deterministic finalization of the InteractionEvent object.

instruction: What public methods are available in the InteractionEvent class?
output: The InteractionEvent class has one public method: Dispose.

instruction: What does the Name property of the InteractionEvent class represent?
output: The Name property of the InteractionEvent class represents the name of the InsertionPoint that was snapped.

instruction: What does the Position property of the InteractionEvent class represent?
output: The documentation does not specify what the Position property represents.

instruction: What are the public properties of the InteractionEvent class?
output: The InteractionEvent class has two public properties: Name and Position.

instruction: What is the purpose of the InternalQueryException class?
output: The InternalQueryException class is thrown when a data model internal query cannot get the result.

instruction: What are the constructors available for the InternalQueryException class?
output: The InternalQueryException class has two constructors: a standard constructor and a constructor that takes a string description.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GetWriteProtectionFlagSet method in the InterruptionPoint class?
output: The GetWriteProtectionFlagSet method checks if a specific write protection kind was set.

instruction: What does the PauseWriteProtection method do in the InterruptionPoint class?
output: The PauseWriteProtection method temporarily disables write protection. Note that current write protection flags are not cleared.

instruction: What are the public methods of the InterruptionPoint class?

instruction: What does the IsStarSource property of the InterruptionPoint class represent?
output: The IsStarSource property gets or sets the Star source property. If true, the interruption point is a star source; otherwise, it is not.

instruction: What does the Properties property of the InterruptionPoint class allow access to?
output: The Properties property allows user access to InterruptionPoint properties.

instruction: What does the WriteProtected property of the InterruptionPoint class do?
output: The WriteProtected property checks if the object is currently write protected or sets manual write protection.

instruction: What is the purpose of the FUNC_BLOCK_FORMAT property in the InterruptionPointPropertyList class?
output: The FUNC_BLOCK_FORMAT property represents the block property format.

instruction: What is the purpose of the FUNC_BLOCK_VALUE property in the InterruptionPointPropertyList class?
output: The FUNC_BLOCK_VALUE property represents the block property value.

instruction: What does the FUNC_MAXCROSSREFERENCEBLOCKCOUNT property represent?

output: The Property property is used by the operator[] to access indexed properties.
