instruction: What does the GetPartNumber method do in the MDPartsMessage class?
output: The GetPartNumber method returns the part number associated with the message, if any.

instruction: What does the GetPartVariant method do in the MDPartsMessage class?
output: The GetPartVariant method returns the part variant associated with the message, if any.

instruction: What does the GetText method do in the MDPartsMessage class?
output: The GetText method returns the complete text of the message.

instruction: What does the GetUser method do in the MDPartsMessage class?
output: The GetUser method returns the computer name of the user who created the message.

instruction: What does the SetDone method do in the MDPartsMessage class?
output: The SetDone method marks the message as solved or not.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MDPartsMessagesEnumerator class?
output: The MDPartsMessagesEnumerator class supports a simple iteration over MDPartsDatabaseMessages.

instruction: What does the MDPartsMessagesEnumerator constructor do?
output: The MDPartsMessagesEnumerator constructor initializes the matching enumerator.

instruction: What does the Init method do in the MDPartsMessagesEnumerator class?
output: The Init method initializes the enumerator for iterating over the collection of all registered messages in the system. There are multiple overloads for this method.

instruction: What does the MoveNext method do in the MDPartsMessagesEnumerator class?
output: The MoveNext method advances the enumerator to the next element of the MDPartsDatabaseMessages. It returns true if the enumerator was successfully advanced to the next element and false if the enumerator has passed the end of the collection.

instruction: What does the Reset method do in the MDPartsMessagesEnumerator class?
output: The Reset method sets the enumerator to its initial position, which is before the first element in the MDPartsDatabaseMessages.

instruction: What does the Current property return in the MDPartsMessagesEnumerator class?
output: The Current property gets the current element in the MDPartsDatabaseMessages.

instruction: What does the CurrentMDPartsMessage property return in the MDPartsMessagesEnumerator class?
output: The CurrentMDPartsMessage property gets the current element in the MDPartsDatabaseMessages.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection class?
output: The MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection class is a collection of all possible message types, registered in the system, that can be used by MDPartsDatabaseMessages.

instruction: What does the constructor of MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection do?
output: The MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection constructor initializes the matching enumerator.

instruction: What does the GetEnumerator method do in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection class?
output: The GetEnumerator method returns an enumerator that can iterate through a collection.

instruction: What does the GetRegisteredPrjMsgsEnumerator method do in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection class?
output: The GetRegisteredPrjMsgsEnumerator method returns a typed enumerator that can iterate through a collection.

instruction: What does the Count property return in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection class?
output: The Count property gets the number of elements contained in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator class?
output: The MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator class iterates over all registered messages of the system.

instruction: What does the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator constructor do?
output: The MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator constructor initializes the matching enumerator.

instruction: What does the Init method do in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator class?
output: The Init method initializes the enumerator for iterating over the collection of all registered messages in the system. There are multiple overloads for this method.

instruction: What does the MoveNext method do in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator class?
output: The MoveNext method advances the enumerator to the next element of the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection. It returns true if the enumerator was successfully advanced to the next element and false if the enumerator has passed the end of the collection.

instruction: What does the Reset method do in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator class?
output: The Reset method sets the enumerator to its initial position, which is before the first element in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection.

instruction: What does the Current property return in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredEnumerator class?
output: The Current property gets the current element in the MDPartsMessagesRegisteredCollection.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MDPartSupportBarPosition class?
output: The MDPartSupportBarPosition class represents the position of a support bar part. Parts of product top group: mechanic, product group: busbar and product sub group: busbar adapter can hold a list of support bar parts These are represented by a MDPartSupportBarPosition object.

instruction: What properties does the MDPartSupportBarPosition class have?
output: The MDPartSupportBarPosition class has the following properties: Length, Part, PartNr, Variant, X, Y, and Z.

instruction: What does the Length property represent in the MDPartSupportBarPosition class?
output: The Length property represents the length of the referenced support bar part.

instruction: What does the Part property represent in the MDPartSupportBarPosition class?
output: The Part property represents the referenced support bar part as an MDPart object.

instruction: What does the PartNr property represent in the MDPartSupportBarPosition class?
output: The PartNr property represents the part number of the referenced assembly part.

instruction: What does the Variant property represent in the MDPartSupportBarPosition class?
output: The Variant property represents the variant of the referenced assembly part.

instruction: What do the X, Y, and Z properties represent in the MDPartSupportBarPosition class?
output: The X, Y, and Z properties represent the x, y, and z positions of the referenced support bar part, respectively.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MDPermissionException class?
output: The MDPermissionException class is the base class for errors caused by missing permission.

instruction: What does the constructor of MDPermissionException do?
output: The MDPermissionException constructor is a standard constructor.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The MDPlcTemplatePosition class represents a PLC function template of a part.

instruction: What properties does the MDPlcTemplatePosition class have?
output: The MDPlcTemplatePosition class has the following properties: BusSystemKind, ChannelDesignation, ConnectionDimension, ConnectionPointCrossSection, ConnectionPointDescription, ConnectionPointDesignation, FunctionDefinitionCategory, FunctionDefinitionGroup, FunctionDefinitionId, HarnessLink, IsIntrinsicallySafe, IsRelevantToSafety, PlcBusInterfaceName, PlcSubdeviceIndex, PlugDeviceTag, SignalRange, Symbol, and SymbolMacro.

instruction: What does the BusSystemKind property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The BusSystemKind property represents the bus system of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the ChannelDesignation property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The ChannelDesignation property represents the channel designation of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the ConnectionDimension property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The ConnectionDimension property represents the connection dimension of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the ConnectionPointCrossSection property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The ConnectionPointCrossSection property represents the connection point cross-section of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the ConnectionPointDescription property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The ConnectionPointDescription property represents the connection point description of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the ConnectionPointDesignation property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The ConnectionPointDesignation property represents the connection point designation of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the IsIntrinsicallySafe property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The IsIntrinsicallySafe property indicates whether the PLC template is intrinsically safe (yes / no).

instruction: What does the IsRelevantToSafety property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The IsRelevantToSafety property indicates whether the PLC template is relevant to safety (yes / no).

instruction: What does the PlcBusInterfaceName property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The PlcBusInterfaceName property represents the PLC bus interface name.

instruction: What does the PlcSubdeviceIndex property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The PlcSubdeviceIndex property represents the PLC subdevice index.

instruction: What does the PlugDeviceTag property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The PlugDeviceTag property represents the plug device tag of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the SignalRange property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The SignalRange property represents the signal range of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the Symbol property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The Symbol property represents the symbol of the PLC template.

instruction: What does the SymbolMacro property represent in the MDPlcTemplatePosition class?
output: The SymbolMacro property represents the symbol macro of the PLC template.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MDPlugTemplatePosition class?
output: The MDPlugTemplatePosition class represents a plug function template of a part.

instruction: What does the class MDSymbolLibraryPropertyList represent?
output: The class MDSymbolLibraryPropertyList represents a collection of P8 properties of the MDSymbolLibrary class.

instruction: What is the ARTICLE_SAFETYRELATED_SILCL property used for?
output: The ARTICLE_SAFETYRELATED_SILCL property is used to store the SIL limit (Safety Integrity Level Claim Limit). It specifies the maximum SIL required for a subsystem. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the ARTICLE_SAFETYRELATED_TMT1 property?
output: The ARTICLE_SAFETYRELATED_TMT1 property returns a value of type System.Double. It represents the duration of use, Mission Time, or proof-test interval.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SALESPRICE_1 property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_SALESPRICE_1 property represents the sales price in Currency 1. The property value is of type System.Double.

instruction: What is the data type of the ARTICLE_SALESPRICE_2 property?
output: The ARTICLE_SALESPRICE_2 property returns a value of type System.Double. It represents the sales price in Currency 2.

instruction: What is the meaning of the ARTICLE_SECONDARY_CASING_PRESSURE_STAGE property?
output: The ARTICLE_SECONDARY_CASING_PRESSURE_STAGE property specifies the pressure level of the secondary housing. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SERVICE_UNIT property describe?
output: The ARTICLE_SERVICE_UNIT property represents the performance unit (bill of quantities). It specifies the measuring unit in which the output to be provided is specified, for example, cubic meter (m³) for concrete work or piece (unit) for individual items such as windows or doors. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What is the ARTICLE_SET_POINT property used for?
output: The ARTICLE_SET_POINT property represents the setpoint in a closed loop control system. It is the target value that the process value is to assume, expressed in units of the process value. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SHOCK_LOAD property describe?
output: The ARTICLE_SHOCK_LOAD property describes the shock load. It represents the current increase as a multiple of the nominal current due to short-term current peaks or voltage peaks. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ARTICLE_SPARE property?
output: The ARTICLE_SPARE property identifies a spare part. It can be, for example, the name of the item that can replace the defective or worn part. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the ARTICLE_SPECIFIED_MAXIMUM_DRIVE_TORQUE property used for?
output: The ARTICLE_SPECIFIED_MAXIMUM_DRIVE_TORQUE property specifies the permissible maximum drive torque for which an item or system is designed. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the ARTICLE_SPECIFIED_MINIMUM_DRIVE_TORQUE property used for?
output: The ARTICLE_SPECIFIED_MINIMUM_DRIVE_TORQUE property specifies the required lowest drive torque to be applied to an item or system in order to perform the task. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SPEED_MAX property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_SPEED_MAX property represents the maximum permitted rotational speed for a tool or a tool holder. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SPEED_MIN property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_SPEED_MIN property represents the minimum possible rotation speed at which a motor can be operated continuously at the thermally permissible rated operating point. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_STANDARD_BACNET_ property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_STANDARD_BACNET_ property represents the standard or international standard valid for BACnet. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_START_UP_TIME property describe?
output: The ARTICLE_START_UP_TIME property describes the switch-on time. It represents the time interval which has elapsed between the application of the control signal and the pressure increase at the corresponding valve outlet until 10% of the specified working pressure is reached. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_STARTING_CURRENT_A property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_STARTING_CURRENT_A property represents the maximum starting current of electrical consumers. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What information does the ARTICLE_STORAGE_TRANSPORT_AND_PACKAGING_REQUIREMENT property provide?
output: The ARTICLE_STORAGE_TRANSPORT_AND_PACKAGING_REQUIREMENT property provides the requirements for logistic processes. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_STRESS property describe?
output: The ARTICLE_STRESS property describes the types of stress, differentiating between mechanical movement values (static and dynamic) and environmental influences (e.g., moisture, heat, chemical reactions). The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_STRIPPING_LENGTH property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_STRIPPING_LENGTH property represents the length of the area on a cable where the outer sheath is removed or can be removed. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SUITABLE_AS_MONITOR property indicate?
output: The ARTICLE_SUITABLE_AS_MONITOR property indicates if a component can be used for temperature monitoring. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SUITABLE_FOR_CABLE_DIAMETERS property describe?
output: The ARTICLE_SUITABLE_FOR_CABLE_DIAMETERS property describes the possible cable diameters for which a specific product or component is suitable. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SUITABLE_FOR_PROTECTION_CLASS_IP property specify?
output: The ARTICLE_SUITABLE_FOR_PROTECTION_CLASS_IP property specifies the classification of the protection of a device or a component against the penetration of foreign objects and water. The property value is of type System.Int64.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SUPPLIER property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_SUPPLIER property represents the short name of the supplier. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SUPPLIER_BATCH_NUMBER property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_SUPPLIER_BATCH_NUMBER property represents a one-time and unique alphanumeric identifier used to uniquely identify products from different manufacturing processes. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SUPPLY_VOLTAGE_RANGE property specify?
output: The ARTICLE_SUPPLY_VOLTAGE_RANGE property specifies the specified value range of the voltage that can be applied to a device for supplying power. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_SWITCHING_CAPACITY property describe?
output: The ARTICLE_SWITCHING_CAPACITY property describes the maximum electrical load that a switching device can switch reliably without damage or malfunctions occurring. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TEMPERATUR_MEDIUM_MAX property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TEMPERATUR_MEDIUM_MAX property represents the maximum temperature that a medium (such as a liquid or a gas) can reach while flowing through a device or component without causing damage or impairing its functionality. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TEMPERATUR_MEDIUM_MIN property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TEMPERATUR_MEDIUM_MIN property represents the minimum temperature that a medium (such as a liquid or a gas) can reach while flowing through a device or component without causing damage or impairing its functionality. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_MAX property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_MAX property represents the maximum operating temperature that a device or component can withstand safely without being damaged or functionality being impaired. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_MIN property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_MIN property represents the minimum operating temperature that a device or component can withstand safely without being damaged or functionality being impaired. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_RANGE_MEDIUM_MAX property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_RANGE_MEDIUM_MAX property represents the maximum temperature range that a medium (such as a liquid or a gas) can reach while flowing through a device or component without causing damage or impairing its functionality. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_RANGE_MEDIUM_MIN property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TEMPERATURE_RANGE_MEDIUM_MIN property represents the minimum temperature range that a medium (such as a liquid or a gas) can reach while flowing through a device or component without causing damage or impairing its functionality. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_THROUGHPUT property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_THROUGHPUT property represents the quantity that is processed or transferred within a specified time period through a predefined limit. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TORQUE_ property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TORQUE_ property represents the measured torque of the motor. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TORQUE_AT_MAX_SPEED property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TORQUE_AT_MAX_SPEED property represents the torque at the maximum permissible output speed of the drive engine. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TORQUE_AT_MIN_SPEED property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TORQUE_AT_MIN_SPEED property represents the torque at the minimum permissible output speed of the drive engine. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TORQUE_MAX_ property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TORQUE_MAX_ property represents the maximum mechanically permissible torque that the motor can output to the output shaft. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TOTAL_NUMBER_OF_BACNET_OBJECTS property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TOTAL_NUMBER_OF_BACNET_OBJECTS property represents the number of all objects or object types in the BACnet protocol. The property value is of type System.Int64.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TYPE_OF_FLOW property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TYPE_OF_FLOW property represents the way in which a medium (e.g., liquid or gas) flows through a system or component. The value can be specified as mass flow or volume flow. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TYPE_OF_SWITCHING property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TYPE_OF_SWITCHING property represents the circuit type. It describes the way in which an electrical device or a component is connected in a circuit. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TYPE_OF_USE property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TYPE_OF_USE property represents the operating mode. It describes the way in which a product or service is used. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_TYPENR property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_TYPENR property represents the type number of the part entered in parts management. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_UNIQUEID property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_UNIQUEID property uniquely identifies the part. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_UNIT property specify?
output: The ARTICLE_UNIT property specifies the measuring unit. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_UNIT_CLASS property specify?
output: The ARTICLE_UNIT_CLASS property specifies the device class. It specifies the degree of protection of a housing and how well a device is protected against electric shocks. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_UNIT_DESIGN property specify?
output: The ARTICLE_UNIT_DESIGN property specifies the type of construction of a device. These specifications are, among other things, relevant for mounting and the operating location. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_UPPER_PROCESS_PRESSURE_LIMIT_ABSOLUTE_PRESSURE property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_UPPER_PROCESS_PRESSURE_LIMIT_ABSOLUTE_PRESSURE property represents the highest absolute process pressure to which the parts of the device that are in contact with the media can be exposed without permanent limitation of the operating behavior. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_UPPER_PROCESS_PRESSURE_LIMIT_GAUGE_PRESSURE property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_UPPER_PROCESS_PRESSURE_LIMIT_GAUGE_PRESSURE property represents the highest process overpressure to which parts of the device that are in contact with the media can be exposed without permanent limitation of the operating behavior. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_USAGE property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_USAGE property represents the procurement. It is the estimated time required to obtain the part. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_USE_FOR_MARKING_TYPE property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_USE_FOR_MARKING_TYPE property represents the way in which a product can be labeled or identified. The property value is of type [Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html).

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_VERSION_AS_MAINTENANCE_REPAIR_SWITCH property specify?
output: The ARTICLE_VERSION_AS_MAINTENANCE_REPAIR_SWITCH property specifies whether the switch version is suitable for use as a maintenance / repair switch on the basis of the technical properties. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_VISCOSITY property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_VISCOSITY property represents the measure for the viscosity of the fluid. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_VISCOSITY_CLASS_ACCORDING_TO_DIN_51519 property specify?
output: The ARTICLE_VISCOSITY_CLASS_ACCORDING_TO_DIN_51519 property specifies which minimum viscosity and which maximum viscosity the respective hydraulic oil has at 40 °C. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_VISCOSITY_INDEX_ACCORDING_TO_DIN_ISO_2909 property describe?
output: The ARTICLE_VISCOSITY_INDEX_ACCORDING_TO_DIN_ISO_2909 property describes a temperature-related kinematic viscosity change of a base oil or a completely formulated oil, but not its actual viscosity. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_VOLUME_FLOW_HEATING_M3_H property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_VOLUME_FLOW_HEATING_M3_H property represents the flow rate. It is the quantity of a medium (such as liquid or gas) which flows through a specific cross-section per unit of time. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_WEAR property identify?
output: The ARTICLE_WEAR property identifies a wearing part, e.g. the position of an item that is especially prone to wear. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_WEIGHT_ITEM property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_WEIGHT_ITEM property represents the weight of the part without packaging and without transport device. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_WEIGHT_KG_1000_M property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_WEIGHT_KG_1000_M property represents the weight of a part per 1000 meters of length. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_WEIGHT_OF_THE_INDIVIDUAL_ARTICLE_PACKAGING property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_WEIGHT_OF_THE_INDIVIDUAL_ARTICLE_PACKAGING property represents the weight of the individual part packaging. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_WEIGHT_OF_THE_PACKAGING property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_WEIGHT_OF_THE_PACKAGING property represents the total weight of the packaging of a product. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLE_WEIGHT_TOTAL property represent?
output: The ARTICLE_WEIGHT_TOTAL property represents the total weight of the part including all relevant components such as housing or supplemental parts. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the ARTICLE_WIDTH property?
output: The ARTICLE_WIDTH property returns a value of type System.Double.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ARTICLEREF_ADDITIONAL_BOOLFIELD property?
output: The ARTICLEREF_ADDITIONAL_BOOLFIELD property is a supplementary field for part reference data. It serves for the entry of free additional properties that can only assume two values ("Yes" or "No"). The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ARTICLEREF_ADDITIONAL_TEXTFIELD property?
output: The ARTICLEREF_ADDITIONAL_TEXTFIELD property is a supplementary field for part reference data. It serves for the entry of free additional properties. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_AML_GUID property store?
output: The ARTICLEREF_AML_GUID property stores the GUIDs for accessory parts in AutomationML AR APC format during the PLC data exchange. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_ARTICLEDEFINITION property indicate?
output: The ARTICLEREF_ARTICLEDEFINITION property indicates if a part is part of a part definition. The property value is of type System.Boolean.

instruction: What is an assembly according to the ARTICLEREF_ASSEMBLY property?
output: According to the ARTICLEREF_ASSEMBLY property, an assembly is a collection of parts that belong to a device (e.g. a pushbutton with a normally open contact, the appropriate mounting and the button). An assembly has its own part number and can also contain (sub) assemblies. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the ARTICLEREF_ASSEMBLYSTRUCTURE property?
output: The ARTICLEREF_ASSEMBLYSTRUCTURE property returns a value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the ARTICLEREF_ASSEMBLYVARIANT property?
output: The ARTICLEREF_ASSEMBLYVARIANT property returns a value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_ASSIGNMENT property define?
output: The ARTICLEREF_ASSIGNMENT property defines whether the assigned part is the main part or an accessory part. The property value is of type System.Int64.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_CABLE_LENGTH_SUM property represent?
output: The ARTICLEREF_CABLE_LENGTH_SUM property represents the total cable length. The property value is of type System.Double.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_CONSTRUCTION_NAME property output?
output: The ARTICLEREF_CONSTRUCTION_NAME property outputs the name of the used drilling pattern. The property value is of type System.String.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_COUNT property represent?
output: The ARTICLEREF_COUNT property represents the number of parts. The property value is of type System.Int64.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_COUNT_NOTPLACED property represent?
output: The ARTICLEREF_COUNT_NOTPLACED property represents the number of unplaced parts. The property value is of type System.Int64.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_COUNT_NOTPLACED_3D property represent?
output: The ARTICLEREF_COUNT_NOTPLACED_3D property represents the number of parts that are available in the project, but that have not been placed in the layout space. The property value is of type System.Int64.

instruction: What does the ARTICLEREF_COUNT_PLACED property represent?
output: The ARTICLEREF_COUNT_PLACED property represents the number of placed parts. The property value is of type System.Int64.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MergedArticleReferencePropertyList class?
output: The MergedArticleReferencePropertyList class represents a collection of properties for the MergedArticleReference class.

instruction: What is the base class of MergedArticleReferencePropertyList?
output: The base class of MergedArticleReferencePropertyList is StorableObjectPropertyList.

instruction: How are the elements of MergedArticleReferencePropertyList accessed?
output: The elements of MergedArticleReferencePropertyList are accessed using the operator[].

instruction: What are some of the public properties available in the MergedArticleReferencePropertyList class?
output: Some of the public properties include ARTICLE_ABSORPTION_VOLUME, ARTICLE_ACTIVE_POWER, ARTICLE_CABLE_LENGTH_MAX, ARTICLE_DESCR1, ARTICLE_ERPNR, and many more related to article properties.

instruction: What does the WriteBackConnectionData method do?
output: The WriteBackConnectionData method writes back the data of merged connections to their corresponding connections.

instruction: What exception is thrown if the objects in the WriteBackConnectionData method belong to more than one project?
output: System.ArgumentException is thrown if the objects in the WriteBackConnectionData method belong to more than one project.

instruction: What is the purpose of the WriteBackFunctionData method?
output: The WriteBackFunctionData method writes back the data of merged functions to their corresponding functions.

instruction: What exception is thrown if the objects in the WriteBackFunctionData method belong to more than one project?
output: System.ArgumentException is thrown if the objects in the WriteBackFunctionData method belong to more than one project.

instruction: What is the purpose of the CreateMergedConnections method?
output: The CreateMergedConnections method returns an array of MergedConnection objects created from the connections passed in the array parameter. Connections that belong to representing the same corresponding connections with different placement types are merged together into one merged connection in the output vector.

instruction: What does the GetMergedProperty method do?
output: The GetMergedProperty method returns the value of a merged property given a property ID (AnyPropertyId).

instruction: What does the IsCovered method do?
output: The IsCovered method checks if a given connection is covered by a MergedConnection object.

instruction: What does the FirstWinner property return?
output: The FirstWinner property returns the first object covered by the merged connection.

instruction: What does the FunctionDefinition property return?
output: The FunctionDefinition property returns the FunctionDefinition of the merged connection, or NULL if the merged connection doesn't have a valid FunctionDefinition.

instruction: What does the MergedObjects property return?
output: The MergedObjects property returns an array of Connections covered by the merged connection.

instruction: What does the NameParts property represent?
output: The NameParts property represents the name of the merged connection, stored in a FunctionBasePropertyList as a list of properties.

instruction: What is the purpose of the CreateMergedFunctions method?
output: The CreateMergedFunctions method returns an array of MergedFunction objects created from the functions passed in the array parameter. Functions that belong to the same device and represent one functional part of the device are merged together into one merged function in the output vector.

instruction: What does the GetMainPlacement method do?
output: The GetMainPlacement method returns the main placement of the MergedFunction by parameters (Page and PointD).

instruction: What does the GetMergedProperty method do in the MergedFunction class?
output: The GetMergedProperty method returns the value of a merged property given a property ID (AnyPropertyId).

instruction: What does the IsCovered method do in the MergedFunction class?
output: The IsCovered method checks if a given StorableObject (function or connection) is covered by the merged function.

instruction: What does the FirstWinner property return in the MergedFunction class?
output: The FirstWinner property returns the first object covered by the merged function.

instruction: What does the FunctionDefinition property return in the MergedFunction class?
output: The FunctionDefinition property returns the FunctionDefinition of the merged function, or NULL if the merged function doesn't have a valid FunctionDefinition.

instruction: What does the MergedObjects property return in the MergedFunction class?
output: The MergedObjects property returns an array of StorableObjects covered by the merged function.

instruction: What does the NameParts property represent in the MergedFunction class?
output: The NameParts property represents the name of the merged function, stored in a FunctionBasePropertyList as a list of properties.

instruction: What does the Properties property provide access to in the MergedFunction class?
output: The Properties property provides access to the P8 properties of the MergedFunction object.

instruction: What does the FaceCount property return in the Mesh class?
output: The FaceCount property returns the number of faces in the mesh.

instruction: What does the FaceNormalVector property return?
output: The FaceNormalVector property returns the normal vector for a face with a given ID.

instruction: What does the FacePoints property return?
output: The FacePoints property returns the points for a given ID of a face.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DoHelp method in the Message class?
output: The DoHelp method is called by EPLAN when the help text for the message should be shown. The method is responsible for calling the appropriate help system with the correct language.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GetMessageText method in the Message class?
output: The GetMessageText method is called by EPLAN when the message text should be shown. The function is responsible for getting the text in the correct language.

instruction: What is the purpose of the OnRegister method in the Message class?
output: The OnRegister method is called by EPLAN when a new project message is added to the system. If a new project message was added to a registered add-in, the add-in must be registered again.

instruction: What does the MessageState property represent?
output: The MessageState property determines the actual adjusted check type.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method in the MountingLineMate class?
output: The Create method creates a mounting line mate.

instruction: What parameters are required for the Create method in the MountingLineMate class?
output: The Create method requires the following parameters: strName (name), pMLSDescription (description), pntStart (start point), and pntEnd (end point).

instruction: What exception is thrown if the strName parameter has zero length in the Create method of the MountingLineMate class?
output: System.ArgumentException is thrown if the strName parameter has zero length in the Create method of the MountingLineMate class.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method that takes Project, double, double, and double parameters in the MountingPanel class?
output: This Create method creates a not placed, article-free MountingPanel object with the specified height, width, and depth.

instruction: What parameters are required for the Create method that takes Project, double, double, and double parameters in the MountingPanel class?
output: The Create method requires the following parameters: oProject (Project), dHeight (height), dWidth (width), and dDepth (depth).

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method that takes Project, string, and string parameters in the MountingPanel class?
output: This Create method creates a not placed MountingPanel object using a part number and variant.

instruction: What parameters are required for the Create method that takes Project, string, and string parameters in the MountingPanel class?
output: The Create method requires the following parameters: oProject (Project), strArticleNr (part number), and strVariant (variant).

instruction: What is the purpose of the CreateTransient method that takes Project, string, and string parameters in the MountingPanel class?
output: This CreateTransient method creates a transient and not placed MountingPanel object using a part number and variant.

instruction: What parameters are required for the CreateTransient method that takes Project, string, and string parameters in the MountingPanel class?
output: The CreateTransient method requires the following parameters: oProject (Project), strArticleNr (part number), and strVariant (variant).

instruction: What is the ARTICLE_MOUNTINGSITE property and what does it do?
output: The ARTICLE_MOUNTINGSITE property is a public property of the MountingPanelPropertyList class. It represents the mounting surface and has the property ID 22022.

instruction: How can I access the existing property IDs of a MountingPanelPropertyList object using the API?
output: You can access the existing property IDs using the ExistingIds property. This property returns an array of AnyPropertyId objects.

instruction: What is the meaning of the FUNC_ARTICLE_WEIGHT_ITEM property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_WEIGHT_ITEM property represents the weight of a part and has the property ID 26371.

instruction: What is the difference between FUNC_ARTICLE_WEIGHT_ITEM and FUNC_ARTICLE_WEIGHT_TOTAL properties?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_WEIGHT_ITEM property represents the weight of a single part, while the FUNC_ARTICLE_WEIGHT_TOTAL property represents the total weight of the part. Both properties are available in the MountingPanelPropertyList.

instruction: What properties are inherited from the Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Placement3DPropertyList class?
output: Many properties are inherited from the Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Placement3DPropertyList class, including properties related to designation, cabling, function, article, and more. Examples include ARTICLE_MOUNTINGSITE, AUTOMATIONML_OBJECTID, DESIGNATION_FULLFUNCTIONALASSIGNMENT, FUNC_ARTICLE_WEIGHT_ITEM, and many others.

instruction: What is the MountingPanelService class used for?
output: The MountingPanelService class provides functionalities related to mounting panels, such as reorganizing legend numbers.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ReorganizeLegendNumbers method?
output: The ReorganizeLegendNumbers method is used to reorganize the legend IDs of a panel layout.

instruction: What are the parameters for the CreateArticlePlacement method that takes a Function, String, and PointD?
output: The CreateArticlePlacement method with the signature CreateArticlePlacement(Function oMountingPanel, string strPartNumber, PointD pLocation) takes the following parameters: oMountingPanel (the mounting panel), strPartNumber (the part number), and pLocation (the location).

instruction: How do I create an article placement on a mounting panel using the CreateArticlePlacement method?
output: You can use the CreateArticlePlacement method.  The article must be in the project's database. The method has several overloads, including one that takes a Function (the mounting panel), a string (the part number), and a PointD (the location).  There are other overloads that allow you to specify the macro variant and article variant.

instruction: What happens if I try to create an article placement with a macro variant that doesn't exist?
output: If you use the CreateArticlePlacement method and specify a macro variant that doesn't exist, a BaseException is thrown.

instruction: What is the effect of the "USER.PanelLayoutGui.Settings.Gripper" setting on the CreateArticlePlacement method?
output: The position of the created article placement is influenced by the part placement handle string setting ("USER.PanelLayoutGui.Settings.Gripper"). If "USER.PanelLayoutGui.Settings.Gripper" is set to "UpperLeft", the created placement will be positioned exactly at the point passed in the pLocation parameter.

instruction: What is the NumberingDirection enum used for in the ReorganizeLegendNumbers method?
output: The NumberingDirection enum is used to specify the numbering direction when reorganizing legend IDs.  The allowed values are Default, Horizontal, and Vertical.

instruction: What is the difference between the Create and CreateTransient methods for MountingRail objects?
output: The Create method creates a persistent MountingRail object, while the CreateTransient method creates a transient MountingRail object. Transient objects are not stored in the project database until explicitly stored.

instruction: What are the parameters required to create a MountingRail object using the Create method?
output: The Create method has overloads that require the following parameters: oProject (the project), strArticleNr (the part number), strVariant (the part variant), and dLength (the length).  There is also an overload that does not take a length, and uses the default length.

instruction: What happens if I provide a null value for the strArticleNr parameter when calling the Create method?
output: If you provide a null value for the strArticleNr parameter when calling the Create method, an ArgumentNullException will be thrown.

instruction: What is the BaseMate property of the MountingRail class used for?
output: The BaseMate property of the MountingRail class represents the default target mate for the MountingRail. When snapping an item by a base mate, it is set as a child of a target Placement3D and the orientation of an item is adjusted to a target Placement3D.

instruction: How can I get the EPLAN properties of a MountingRail object using the API?
output: You can access the EPLAN properties of a MountingRail object through the Properties property, which is of type MountingRailPropertyList.

instruction: What is the MountingRail class in the EPLAN API?
output: The MountingRail class represents a 3D object of a mounting rail in P8. It inherits from BarBase and implements the interfaces IArticleUser, IFunctionBase, and IPropertyPlacementsContainer.

instruction: What is the inheritance hierarchy of the MountingRail class?
output: The MountingRail class inherits from the following classes: System.Object, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.StorableObject, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Placement3D, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Function3D, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.BarBase.

instruction: What is the corresponding FunctionCategory for the MountingRail class?
output: The corresponding FunctionCategory for the MountingRail class is CabMechBodyAccessoryInside.

instruction: How can I create a new MountingRail object using the API?
output: You can create a new, not placed MountingRail object using the static method Create() or CreateTransient() of the MountingRail class.  The Create() method creates a not placed MountingRail object with a given length. The CreateTransient() method creates a transient and not placed MountingRail object with a given length.

instruction: What are the public properties of the MountingRail class?
output: The public properties of the MountingRail class include: AbsoluteTransformation, AbsoluteTransformationOfMacro, AreConnectionPointPositionsLocal, ArticleReferences, Articles, BaseMate, CanHaveArticleData, Children, Color, Connection3Ds, ConnectionPointPositions, Corners, CrossReferencedObjectsAll, DatabaseIdentifier, FunctionDefinition, Group3D, InstallationSpace, IsFixedDevice, IsHidden, IsLocked, IsMeshCreated, IsNameEmpty, IsPlaced, IsReadOnly, IsTransient, IsValid, ItemType, Layer, Length, Mesh, Name, NameParts, ObjectIdentifier, Parent, PlacementArea, Planes, PlanningSegment, Project, Properties, PropertyPlacements, PropertyPlacementsSchemas, RelativeTransformation, RelativeTransformationOfMacro, TopLevelParent, TypeIdentifier, UseLocalPropertyPlacements, VisibleName, and VisibleNameParts.

instruction: What is the BaseMate property used for in the MountingRail class?
output: The BaseMate property defines the default target mate for the MountingRail object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Properties property in the MountingRail class?
output: The Properties property in the MountingRail class provides access to the EPLAN properties of the MountingRail object.

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the Property(AnyPropertyId) property of MountingRailPropertyList?
output: The Property(AnyPropertyId) property can throw the following exceptions: Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException and Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.PropertyNotFoundException.

instruction: What is the purpose of the MountingRailPropertyList constructor that takes a MountingRail object as a parameter?
output: The MountingRailPropertyList constructor that takes a MountingRail object as a parameter should be used internally only. It affects the properties of the provided MountingRail object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32) property in the MountingRailPropertyList class?
output: The Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32) property is used by the operator[] to access indexed properties by AnyPropertyId. It allows you to get or set the value of a property at a specific index.

instruction: What does the class MountingRailPropertyList represent?
output: The class MountingRailPropertyList represents a collection of properties of the MountingRail class.

instruction: What is the inheritance hierarchy of MountingRailPropertyList?
output: The inheritance hierarchy is: System.Object -> Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.UniversalPropertyList -> Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.StorableObjectPropertyList -> Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Placement3DPropertyList -> Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.Function3DPropertyList -> Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.BarBasePropertyList -> Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.MountingRailPropertyList

instruction: What is the purpose of the operator[] in the MountingRailPropertyList class?
output: The operator[] is used to access the elements (stored properties) of the MountingRailPropertyList.

instruction: How can I create a persistent property list using the MountingRailPropertyList class?
What is the difference between online and offline property lists in the context of MountingRailPropertyList?
output: If a property list is 'online', it is associated with properties of some StorableObject or other property list.  If any property is added, changed, or removed from an online property list, the result is also visible in related objects. Whether a property list is online or offline is determined at the time of its creation and cannot be changed.

instruction: What is the purpose of the NameService class in the EPLAN API?
output: The NameService class is used for managing function names (device tags) on a specified Page.

instruction: How do I create a new NameService object in C#?
output: You can create a NameService object using the default constructor `new NameService()` or the constructor that takes a Page object: `new NameService(oPage)`.

instruction: What are the main methods available in the NameService class?
output: The main methods in the NameService class include: AdjustFullName, AdjustVisibleName, CanDeviceTagBeNested, CorrectDeviceTagProperties, EvaluateAndSetAllNames, EvaluateAndSetAllNamesTXN, EvaluateAndSetAllVisibleNames, EvaluateName, FindNameGivingFunction, FindNameGivingObject, GetSortedListOfLogicals, RenameDevice, SetFullNameAndAdjustVisibleName, SetName, SetVisibleNameAndAdjustFullName, and UpdateConnectionDefinitionPointsParent.

instruction: What is the purpose of the AdjustmentOfDeviceTagSeparatorIsEnabled property?
output: The AdjustmentOfDeviceTagSeparatorIsEnabled property determines if the adjustment of the visible device tag separator should be forced.

instruction: What happens if I set the Page property of a NameService object to null?
output: The Page property gets/sets the page. Page must be set, because most of the NameService class's methods work on page only (except for e.g. CorrectDeviceTagProperties, EvaluateAndSetAllNamesTXN methods).

instruction: What is the RemovalOfUnnecessaryDeviceTagNamePartsIsEnabled property used for?
output: The RemovalOfUnnecessaryDeviceTagNamePartsIsEnabled property determines if the removal of unnecessary main and nested device tags in the visible name should be forced.

instruction: When should I use the EvaluateAndSetAllNamesTXN method?
output: The EvaluateAndSetAllNamesTXN method should be used to evaluate all names for all FunctionBase objects in the project. This method does its own transactions, so no transactions around the method are allowed.

instruction: What is the difference between RenameDevice and RenameFunction methods in NameService3D?
output: The RenameDevice method changes the full names of the *found device*, while the RenameFunction method changes the full names of the *function and its children*.  RenameDevice changes the name for all objects with the same full name, while RenameFunction only changes the name for the given Function3D and its children.

instruction: How do I set a default device tag to a function using the NameService3D API?
output: You can set a default device tag to a function using the SetName method.  The function must have its function definition configured (since the code letter is provided by this).

instruction: What are the parameters for the AddDaisyChain method of the NetDefinitionPoint class?
output: The AddDaisyChain method takes an array of NetConnectionPoint objects as a parameter: `NetDefinitionPoint.NetConnectionPoint[] netConnectionPoints`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the AdditionalConnections property of the NetDefinitionPoint class?
output: The AdditionalConnections property provides access to additional connections of the net that are not stored at the NetDefinitionPoint.

instruction: What are the possible values for the NetbasedRoutingSymbols enumeration?
output: The NetbasedRoutingSymbols enumeration has three members: NO, READ_FROM_SETTINGS, and YES.

instruction: What does the ToString method of the NetConnectionPoint class return?
output: The ToString method of the NetConnectionPoint class returns a string that represents the current object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the IdForNetBasedConnections property of the NetConnectionPoint class?
output: The IdForNetBasedConnections property returns the ID for net-based connections.

instruction: What is the purpose of the NetDefinitionPointPropertyList constructor?
output: The NetDefinitionPointPropertyList constructor is used internally and affects the properties of a NetDefinitionPoint object.

instruction: What properties are available in the NetDefinitionPointPropertyList?
output: The NetDefinitionPointPropertyList includes properties like AdditionalConnections, Connections, DaisyChains, NetConnectionPoints, Properties, and properties inherited from PotentialDefinitionPropertyList, SymbolReferencePropertyList, PlacementPropertyList, and StorableObjectPropertyList.

instruction: What does the AddDaisyChain method of the NetDefinitionPoint class do?
output: The AddDaisyChain method creates a new DaisyChain object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the NoLockingStepException?
output: The NoLockingStepException is thrown when any LockingStep object wasn't created to allow restoring the original state of locking.

instruction: What is the purpose of the NOPLCCPU exception?
output: The NOPLCCPU exception is thrown when no Plc CPU is found in the project. Functions from the PlcService classes use this exception.

instruction: When is the NoPropertyListException thrown?
output: The NoPropertyListException is thrown when a data model object does not have a property list in its current state.

instruction: What is the purpose of the NoSuchPinException?
output: The NoSuchPinException is thrown when the user tries to set PlcTrackingOneBased on a pin that doesn't exist.

instruction: What does the NotIndexedPropertyException indicate?
output: The NotIndexedPropertyException is thrown when a user tries to fetch a non-indexed property using an index.

instruction: What is the meaning of the Property field in the NotIndexedPropertyException?
output: The Property field in the NotIndexedPropertyException indicates the property that was used with an index.

instruction: When is the ObjectAlreadyCreatedException thrown?
output: The ObjectAlreadyCreatedException is thrown when a DataModel object has already been created and is created again.

instruction: What does the OperationCanceledException signify?
output: The OperationCanceledException is thrown when a user (or something else) cancels an operation. For example, this can happen when the user presses the Cancel button on a progress bar.

instruction: When is the OperationFailedException thrown?
output: The OperationFailedException is thrown when an operation on a database object has failed.

instruction: What does the AddOptionFragment method do?
output: The AddOptionFragment method adds a fragment to the option.

instruction: What does the Active property of the Option class control?
output: The Active property controls the state of the Option. It is set to true if the option is active, and false if it is not active.

instruction: What is the purpose of the IsTransparent property of the Option class?
output: The IsTransparent property determines if the Option is transparent.

instruction: What does the Remove method of the Option class do?
output: The Remove method removes the Option.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method of the OptionFragment class?
output: The Create method creates the OptionFragment.

instruction: What does the StorableObjects property of the OptionFragment class return?
output: The StorableObjects property returns all objects contained in the OptionFragment.

instruction: What is the purpose of the OPTION_DESCR property in the OptionFragmentPropertyList?
output: The OPTION_DESCR property represents the project option description and is of type MultiLangString.

instruction: What does the AddOption method do in the OptionGroup class?
output: The AddOption method adds an Option to the OptionGroup. The description is set to an empty multilang value, and the state is set to true (so the option is active).

instruction: What does the GetOptionByName method do in the OptionGroup class?
output: The GetOptionByName method returns the Option assigned to the OptionGroup with the given name.

instruction: What does the Options property of the OptionGroup class return?
output: The Options property returns an array of Options assigned to the OptionGroup.

instruction: What is the purpose of the OPTIONGROUP_DESCR property in the OptionGroupPropertyList?
output: The OPTIONGROUP_DESCR property represents the project option group description and is of type MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ConvertToGraphics method of the Page class?
output: The ConvertToGraphics method converts all symbols and connections on the page into graphical instances. Graphical instances of a given symbol are automatically grouped.

instruction: What does the Create method of the Page class do?
output: The Create method creates a new Page object. It is placed in the project under the location given as parameters.

instruction: What does the GetBoundingBox method of the Page class return?
output: The GetBoundingBox method returns the placement bounding box. The bounding box is a rectangle that contains the placement. It can also be used to determine the placement size.

instruction: What does the SetName method of the Page class do?
output: The SetName method sets the name of the page. The name parts should be given in the PagePropertyList.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PageType property of the Page class?
output: The PageType property gets or sets a page's property describing the type of document.

instruction: What does the MacroRepresentationType property of the Page class represent?
output: The MacroRepresentationType property describes the type of macro for the page. This type is used to find a suitable variant of macro, which can be inserted on this page. This property depends on the PageType.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Filter property of the Page class?
output: The Filter property allows access to the current page's filter. It affects the Functions, Terminals, TerminalStrips, Plugs, PlugStrips, and PLCs properties.

instruction: What does the AllFirstLevelPlacements property of the Page class return?
output: The AllFirstLevelPlacements property returns all Placements, including Groups, placed on the page. They are not filtered. All returned Placements are placed directly on this page. This method does not return embedded objects.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method in the PageMacro class?
output: The Create method writes a macro file from the pages and then, if there was at least one valid page, opens the created macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Open method in the PageMacro class?
output: The Open method in the PageMacro class opens a macro file and retrieves information from it. It takes the macro file name (including path and type) and the related project as parameters.

instruction: What are the parameters of the Open method in the PageMacro class?
output: The Open method in the PageMacro class takes two parameters: macroFileName (string) which is the filename (inclusive path information and type) of the macro, and oProject (Project) which is the related project.

instruction: What exception can be thrown by the Open method of the PageMacro class?
output: The Open method of the PageMacro class can throw a System.ArgumentException if the parameters are not valid, for example, if the filename is incorrect.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Pages property in the PageMacro class?
output: The Pages property in the PageMacro class returns the pages that are contained in the macro. It is an array of pages.

instruction: What is the return type of the Pages property in the PageMacro class?
output: The Pages property in the PageMacro class returns an array of Page objects.

instruction: What does the PlaceHolders property of the PageMacro class return?
output: The PlaceHolders property of the PageMacro class returns all Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Graphics.PlaceHolder objects contained in the macro.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PageMacro.Enums class?
output: The PageMacro.Enums class is used only as a container for enumerations, like a namespace.

instruction: What is the purpose of the NumerationMode enumeration within the PageMacro.Enums class?
output: The NumerationMode enumeration within the PageMacro.Enums class specifies the insertion mode when inserting a macro via the Eplan.EplApi.HeServices.Insert class.

instruction: What are the possible values of the NumerationMode enumeration?
output: The possible values of the NumerationMode enumeration are: Ignore (0), None (1), Number (2), NumberWithQuestionMark (3), and NumberPreferPrefix (4).

instruction: What does the PagePropertyList class represent?
output: The PagePropertyList class represents a collection of properties of the Page class.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_DOCTYPE property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_DOCTYPE property in the PagePropertyList class represents the document type (main identifier) with ID 1500. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the return type of the DESIGNATION_DOCTYPE_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_DOCTYPE_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_FULLDOCTYPE property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_FULLDOCTYPE property in the PagePropertyList class represents the document type with ID 1520. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_FULLFUNCTIONALASSIGNMENT property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_FULLFUNCTIONALASSIGNMENT property in the PagePropertyList class provides the full functional assignment with ID 1320. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the EDITINGAREA_CRAFT property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The EDITINGAREA_CRAFT property in the PagePropertyList class represents the trade (defined working sections) with ID 25000. It returns a property value of type System.Int64.

instruction: What are the possible values for the EDITINGAREA_CRAFT property?
output: The possible values for the EDITINGAREA_CRAFT property are: 0 = Electrical engineering, 1 = Fluid power, general, 2 = Mechanics, 3 = Process engineering, and 99 = General.

instruction: What is the purpose of the FRAME_PATH_STARTID property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The FRAME_PATH_STARTID property in the PagePropertyList class represents the start value (column) with ID 12025. It returns a property value of type System.Int64.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_AUTO_CREATED_INFO property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_AUTO_CREATED_INFO property in the PagePropertyList class indicates whether the page has been automatically generated and provides information about the author with ID 11006. It returns a property value of type System.Int64.

instruction: What are the possible values for the PAGE_AUTO_CREATED_INFO property?
output: The possible values for the PAGE_AUTO_CREATED_INFO property are: 0 = No, 1 = Single-line representation (PLC schematic generation), 2 = Multi-line representation (PLC schematic generation), 3 = I/O overview (PLC schematic generation), 4 = Rack overview (PLC schematic generation), 5 = Eplan Engineering Configuration, 6 = Schematic generator (ESG), 7 = Report, and 8 = Pre-planning.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_COUNTER property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_COUNTER property in the PagePropertyList class represents the page counter with ID 11012. It returns a property value of type System.String. This property can be used in forms but is not designed for use in filters.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_CRAFT property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_CRAFT property in the PagePropertyList class determines the technology to be applied or selected (trade) with ID 11037. It returns a property value of type System.Int64.

instruction: What are the possible values for the PAGE_CRAFT property?
output: The possible values for the PAGE_CRAFT property are: 0 = Electrical engineering, 1 = Mechanics, 2 = Hydraulics, 3 = Pneumatics, 4 = Cooling, 5 = Lubrication, 6 = Process engineering, 7 = Fluid power, general, and 99 = General.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_CREATOR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_CREATOR property in the PagePropertyList class displays the sign-in name or the combination of the user code and user name of the creator of the page with ID 11020. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_FORMPLOT property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_FORMPLOT property in the PagePropertyList class represents the plot frame name with ID 11016. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_FORMULAR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_FORMULAR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the form name with ID 11015. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_FULLNAME property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_FULLNAME property in the PagePropertyList class represents the full page name with ID 11009. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_IDENTNAME property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_IDENTNAME property in the PagePropertyList class represents the identifying page name with ID 11010. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_NAME property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_NAME property in the PagePropertyList class represents the page name with ID 11000. It returns a property value of type System.String. It consists of the page number, i.e. the page counter and subcounter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_NUMBER property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_NUMBER property in the PagePropertyList class represents the page number with ID 11042. It returns a property value of type System.String. It is part of the page name and consists of the page counter and the page's subcounter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_OBJECTCODE property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_OBJECTCODE property in the PagePropertyList class represents the object identifier with ID 11036. It returns a property value of type System.String. It is used for the identification of pages in accordance with DIN EN 61355-1.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_TYPE property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_TYPE property in the PagePropertyList class defines the page type with ID 11017. It returns a property value of type System.String. It defines whether it is a page for schematics, mounting panels, terminal diagrams, etc.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PAGE_TYPE_NUMERIC property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The PAGE_TYPE_NUMERIC property in the PagePropertyList class defines the page type (ID) with ID 11029. It returns a property value of type System.Int64. It defines the page type, i.e. whether it is a page for schematics, mounting panels, terminal diagrams, etc.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure with ID 1600. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure: Description with ID 1650. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB1 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB1 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 1) with ID 1601. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB1_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB1_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 1): Description with ID 1631. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB2 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB2 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 2) with ID 1602. It returns a property value of type System.String.
