output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB2_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 2): Description with ID 1632. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB3 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB3 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 3) with ID 1603. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB3_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB3_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 3): Description with ID 1633. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB4 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB4 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 4) with ID 1604. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB4_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB4_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 4): Description with ID 1634. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB5 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB5 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 5) with ID 1605. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB5_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB5_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 5): Description with ID 1635. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB6 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB6 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 6) with ID 1606. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB6_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB6_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 6): Description with ID 1636. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB7 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB7 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 7) with ID 1607. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB7_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB7_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 7): Description with ID 1637. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB8 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB8 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 8) with ID 1608. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB8_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB8_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 8): Description with ID 1638. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB9 property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB9 property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 9) with ID 1609. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB9_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The DESIGNATION_USERDEFINED_SUB9_DESCR property in the PagePropertyList class represents the user-defined structure (sub-identifier 9): Description with ID 1639. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Property property in the PagePropertyList class?
output: The Property property in the PagePropertyList class is used by the operator[] in order to access indexed properties. It has multiple overloads to access properties by different identifiers.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PagesFilter class?
output: The PagesFilter class represents a filter for Page and derived classes. It can be accessed as a property of a Project object or used as a parameter for DMObjectsFinder.

instruction: What happens if you set more than one criterion of matching the filter in PagesFilter?
output: Setting more than one criterion of matching the filter (for example Page.PageType and Page.Name), causes that returned pages must match both conditions.

instruction: What is the purpose of the DocumentType property in the PagesFilter class?
output: The DocumentType property in the PagesFilter class returns the Page.PageType that was set to this filter.

instruction: What does the ExactNameMatching property in the PagesFilter class do?
output: The ExactNameMatching property in the PagesFilter class gets if the filtered function, when is filtered by name, should be matched exactly. If the searched name is only its name' prefix, it is not matching to the filter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Name property in the PagesFilter class?
output: The Name property in the PagesFilter class returns the name that was set to this filter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PartsService class?
output: The PartsService class provides parts list and parts management functionality.

instruction: What are the possible values for the PartsService.Format enumeration?
output: The possible values for the PartsService.Format enumeration are: XML (0) and CSV (1).

instruction: What is the purpose of the PartsService.PartInfo struct?
output: The PartsService.PartInfo struct holds basic part descriptions.

instruction: What properties does the PartsService.PartInfo struct have?
output: The PartsService.PartInfo struct has the following properties: partNumber (string), partVariant (string), and nCount (uint).

instruction: What is the purpose of the IsPartsSelectionOnProject method in the PartsService class?
output: The IsPartsSelectionOnProject method in the PartsService class checks if the project's parts selection is set to project. It takes a Project object and a string (passed by reference) as parameters and returns a boolean value.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ReSubstitutePath method?
output: The ReSubstitutePath method substitutes values with variables and returns the changed path.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ReSubstitutePathForDisplay method?
output: The ReSubstitutePathForDisplay method substitutes values with variables and returns the changed path. It ignores all variables which are not visible in P8 Dialogs.

instruction: What is the purpose of the SubstitutePath method?
output: The SubstitutePath method substitutes variables with their values and returns the changed path.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method in the PathText class?
output: The Create method in the PathText class creates the Text object. It has two overloads, one that takes a MultiLangString and another that takes a string as the contents.

instruction: What are the parameters of the Create method in the PathText class when using a MultiLangString?
output: When using a MultiLangString, the Create method in the PathText class takes the following parameters: page ([Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Page](Eplan.EplApi.DataModelu~Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Page.html)), contents ([Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html)), and textHeight (double).

instruction: What are the parameters of the Create method in the PathText class when using a string?
output: When using a string, the Create method in the PathText class takes the following parameters: page ([Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Page](Eplan.EplApi.DataModelu~Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Page.html)), contents (string), and textHeight (double).

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the Create method in the PathText class?
output: The Create method in the PathText class can throw the following exceptions: System.ArgumentNullException (when page or contents is null), Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectCreationException, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectAlreadyCreatedException, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.IncorrectObjectTypeException, and Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.InvalidArgumentException (when given [Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Page](Eplan.EplApi.DataModelu~Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Page.html) has PageType sets to ExternalDocument).

instruction: What does the CreateTransient method in the PathText class do?
output: The CreateTransient method in the PathText class creates a transient and not placed PathText object.

instruction: What are the parameters of the CreateTransient method in the PathText class?
output: The CreateTransient method in the PathText class takes the following parameters: oProject ([Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Project](Eplan.EplApi.DataModelu~Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.Project.html)), contents ([Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString](Eplan.EplApi.Baseu~Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.html)), and textHeight (double).

instruction: What exceptions can be thrown by the CreateTransient method in the PathText class?
output: The CreateTransient method in the PathText class can throw the following exceptions: System.ArgumentNullException (when oProject or contents is null), Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectCreationException, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectAlreadyCreatedException, and Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.IncorrectObjectTypeException.

instruction: What is the purpose of the CreateTransient method in the PathText class?
output: The CreateTransient method creates a transient and not placed PathText object.

instruction: What are the differences between the two overloads of the CreateTransient method?
output: The CreateTransient method has two overloads: one that creates a transient PathText object and another that creates a transient Text object (inherited from the Text class).

instruction: What does the ToPathText method do?
output: The ToPathText method transforms a Text instance into a PathText instance.

instruction: What happens to the original Text object after calling the ToPathText method?
output: After calling the ToPathText method, the original Text object becomes invalid (i.e., deleted).

instruction: What exception is thrown if you call ToPathText on an object that is already a PathText instance?
output: An ObjectCreationException is thrown.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ToText method?
output: The ToText method transforms a PathText instance into a Text instance.

instruction: What happens to the original PathText object after calling the ToText method?
output: After calling the ToText method, the original PathText object becomes invalid (i.e., deleted).

instruction: List some of the public methods available for the PathText class.
output: Some public methods for the PathText class include CopyTo, Create, CreateTransient, Dispose(), DockText, Equals, GetBoundingBox, GetDisplayString, GetHashCode, GetTypeName, LockObject, Remove, Scale, SetVisibilityDependingOnLayer, SmartLock, ToPathText, ToStringIdentifier, ToText, and UndockText.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PathText constructor?
output: The PathText constructor is used to create a PathText object. After creating an object with this constructor, its `create` method must be called.

instruction: How do you access the P8 properties of a PathText object using the API?
output: You can access the P8 properties of a PathText object through the Properties property, which is of type PathTextPropertyList.

instruction: What is the base class for the PathText class?
output: The PathText class inherits from the Text class.

instruction: What are path function texts used for in EPLAN?
output: Path function texts ease documentation because you don't have to enter a function text for each component. These texts can be positioned arbitrarily within a path and are evaluated when generating PLC cross-references, in terminal diagrams, and in bills of materials.

instruction: How can you determine if a function text on a component is entered or automatically generated?
output: Each function has the properties Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.FunctionPropertyList.FUNC_TEXT and Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.FunctionPropertyList.FUNC_TEXT_AUTOMATIC.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Exists method in the PathTextPropertyList class?
output: The Exists method checks property existence for a used object.

instruction: What are the two constructors available for the PathTextPropertyList class?
output: The PathTextPropertyList class has two constructors: a default constructor and a constructor that takes a PathText object as a parameter.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PATHTEXT_FUNCTIONS property?
output: The PATHTEXT_FUNCTIONS property lists all the functions for a path function text that adopt this function text from the path.

instruction: How do you access properties of a PathText object using the Property property in PathTextPropertyList?
output: You can access properties using the Property property, which is overloaded to accept different identifiers (Properties.PathText id, AnyPropertyId propertyId, and their indexed versions).

instruction: What is the base class for the PCTLoop class?
output: The PCTLoop class inherits from the PlanningSegment class.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PCTLoop class?
output: The PCTLoop class represents a PCT-Loop in EPLAN.

instruction: How can you change the name of a PCT-Loop using the API?
output: The names of PCT-Loops are composed of 4 properties. To change the name, change the 4 properties: DMPLAOBJECT_PCT_IDENTIFIER, DMPLAOBJECT_PCT_MEASURAND, DMPLAOBJECT_PCT_FUNCTION, and DMPLAOBJECT_PCT_NUMBER.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Properties property in the PCTLoop class?
output: The Properties property provides access to the EPLAN properties of the PCTLoop object.

instruction: Give an example of how to access and set a property of a Function object using the Properties property.
What is the base class for the PCTLoopFunction class?
output: The PCTLoopFunction class inherits from the PlanningSegment class.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Properties property in the PCTLoopFunction class?
output: The Properties property provides access to the EPLAN properties of the PCTLoopFunction object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PCTLoopFunctionPropertyList constructor?
output: The PCTLoopFunctionPropertyList constructor is used to create a PCTLoopFunctionPropertyList object. It has two overloads, one default and one that takes a PCTLoopFunction object as a parameter.

instruction: What does the class PCTLoopPropertyList represent in the EPLAN API?
output: The `PCTLoopPropertyList` class represents a collection of properties of the `PCTLoop` class.

instruction: What is the base class of PCTLoopPropertyList?
output: The base class of `PCTLoopPropertyList` is `PlanningSegmentPropertyList`.

instruction: List some of the public properties inherited by the PCTLoopPropertyList class.

`DESIGNATION_FULLPLANT_WITHPREFIX`, `DESIGNATION_FULLPRODUCT`, `DESIGNATION_FULLPRODUCT_DESCR`, `DESIGNATION_FULLPRODUCT_WITHPREFIX`, `DESIGNATION_FULLSUBDOCTYPE`, instruction: What is the purpose of the Equality operator for the Pen class?
output: The Equality operator (`==`) for the `Pen` class is used to compare two `Pen` objects. The comparison is made by comparing each of the `Pen`'s members instead of the internal object ID.

instruction: What does the Dispose() method do for the Pen class?
output: The `Dispose()` method for the `Pen` class is a destructor used for the deterministic finalization of the `Pen` object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GetHashCode() method in the Pen class?
output: The `GetHashCode()` method in the `Pen` class serves as the default hash function for the object.

instruction: What are the predefined line color index values for the ColorId property of the Pen class?
output: The predefined line color index values for the `ColorId` property are: 0 = black, 1 = red, 2 = yellow, 3 = green, 4 = cyan, 5 = blue, 6 = magenta, 7 = white, 252 = darkgray, 253 = gray.

instruction: What are the predefined line type index values for the StyleId property of the Pen class?
output: The predefined line type index values for the `StyleId` property are: 0 = continous, 1 = dash, 2 = dot, 3 = dashdot, 4 = dashdotdot, 5 = dash, 6 = dashlongdot.

instruction: What is the range of valid values for the Width property of the Pen class?
output: The valid range for the `Width` property of the `Pen` class is normally 0.13 to 2.

instruction: What is the purpose of the IsExtern property of the Pin class?
output: The `IsExtern` property of the `Pin` class is used to qualify whether the pin is external or internal.

instruction: What does the Designation property of the Pin class return?
output: The `Designation` property of the `Pin` class returns the internal designation of the pin.

instruction: What does the Connections property of the Pin class return?
output: The `Connections` property of the `Pin` class returns connections which start or end at this connection point. It returns `NULL` if this Pin object has been obtained from a FunctionDefinition object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the GetDiameterWithUnit method in the PipingDefinition class?
output: The `GetDiameterWithUnit` method in the `PipingDefinition` class gets the cross-section / diameter with the unit of the object.

instruction: What does the PipeClass property of the PipingDefinition class represent?
output: The `PipeClass` property of the `PipingDefinition` class represents the pipe class of the object.

instruction: What does the SubstanceState property of the PipingDefinition class represent?
output: The `SubstanceState` property of the `PipingDefinition` class represents the substance state of the object. It sets the same property as `PotentialDefinition.SignalName`.

instruction: What is the purpose of the AddRecord method in the PlaceHolder class?
output: The `AddRecord` method in the `PlaceHolder` class adds a new record with the specified name to the PlaceHolder object.

instruction: What does the ApplyRecord method do in the PlaceHolder class?
output: The `ApplyRecord` method in the `PlaceHolder` class applies a record of values on a PlaceHolder object.

instruction: What does the CreateTransient method do in the PlaceHolder class?
output: The `CreateTransient` method in the `PlaceHolder` class creates a transient and not placed PlaceHolder object.

instruction: What does the DeleteRecord method do in the PlaceHolder class?
output: The `DeleteRecord` method in the `PlaceHolder` class deletes a record.

instruction: What does the GetRecordNames method return in the PlaceHolder class?
output: The `GetRecordNames` method returns the names of all records in the nIndex-th PlaceHolder of a Macro.

instruction: What does the GetValue method do in the PlaceHolder class?
output: The `GetValue` method in the `PlaceHolder` class gets the value of a variable for a record.

instruction: What does the IsVariableValid method do in the PlaceHolder class?
output: The `IsVariableValid` method in the `PlaceHolder` class verifies the correctness if a variable name of a placeholder. If a variable name contains invalid characters, this method returns false.

instruction: What does the Remove(Variable) method do in the PlaceHolder.VariablesClass?
output: The Remove(Variable) method removes a variable from the PlaceHolder.VariablesClass. It accepts a PlaceHolder.Variable object as a parameter and returns a boolean value indicating success or failure.

instruction: What are the parameters of the Remove(Variable) method?
output: The Remove(Variable) method takes a single parameter: item, which is of type PlaceHolder.Variable.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Remove method in PlaceHolder.VariablesClass?
output: The Remove method in PlaceHolder.VariablesClass is used to remove a variable. It is overloaded and has two versions: Remove(String) and Remove(Variable).

instruction: List the public methods available in the PlaceHolder.VariablesClass.
output: The public methods in PlaceHolder.VariablesClass are: Add, Clear, Contains, CopyTo, GetEnumerator, GetEnumerator2, and Remove.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PlaceHolder.VariablesClass constructor?
output: The PlaceHolder.VariablesClass constructor initializes a new instance of the PlaceHolder.VariablesClass. It takes a PlaceHolder object as a parameter.

instruction: What does the _Displayed property return?
output: The _Displayed property returns displayed values as a string array.

instruction: What does the Count property return in PlaceHolder.VariablesClass?
output: The Count property returns the number of variables stored in the PlaceHolder.VariablesClass.

instruction: What does the IsReadOnly property return?
output: The IsReadOnly property returns a boolean value indicating whether the PlaceHolder.VariablesClass is read-only.

instruction: What are the overloads for the Item property in PlaceHolder.VariablesClass?
output: The Item property in PlaceHolder.VariablesClass is overloaded. It can be accessed using either an integer index (Item(Int32)) or a string name (Item(String)).

instruction: What does the AddRecord method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The AddRecord method adds a new record with the specified name to the Placeholder3D object. It takes a string strRecordName as a parameter and returns the index of the record.

instruction: What does the AddReference method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The AddReference method adds the reference of a Placement3D object to the Placeholder3D. It takes a Placement3D object (pObject) as a parameter.

instruction: What does the AddVariable method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The AddVariable method adds a new variable to the Placeholder3D object. It takes a string strVarName as a parameter and returns the index of the variable.

instruction: What are the parameters of the ApplyRecord(String, Boolean) method?
output: The ApplyRecord(String, Boolean) method takes two parameters: strRecordName (the name of the record to apply) and bApplyPageAndProjectData (a boolean indicating whether to set the page and project data).

instruction: What exceptions can the ApplyRecord(String, Boolean) method throw?
output: The ApplyRecord(String, Boolean) method can throw the following exceptions: System.ArgumentNullException, System.ArgumentException, and System.ApplicationException.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Create method in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The Create method creates a new PlaceHolder3D object on the specified InstallationSpace. It takes an InstallationSpace object as a parameter.

instruction: What are the parameters of the CreateTransient method in PlaceHolder3D?
output: The CreateTransient method takes a Project object (oProject) as a parameter.

instruction: What exceptions can the CreateTransient method throw?
output: The CreateTransient method can throw Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectCreationException and Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectAlreadyCreatedException.

instruction: What does the DeleteRecord(Int32) method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The DeleteRecord(Int32) method deletes a record from the Placeholder3D object. It takes an integer iPos (index of the record) as a parameter.

instruction: What exceptions can the DeleteRecord(Int32) method throw?
output: The DeleteRecord(Int32) method can throw System.ArgumentException.

instruction: What does the DeleteUnusedVariables method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The DeleteUnusedVariables method deletes all unused variables from the Placeholder3D object.

instruction: What does the DeleteVariable method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The DeleteVariable method deletes a variable from the Placeholder3D object. It takes a string strVarName (name of a variable) as a parameter.

instruction: What does the FindRecord method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The FindRecord method finds a record by name within the Placeholder3D object. It takes a string strName (name of the record) as a parameter and returns the index of the record or -1 if not found.

instruction: What does the FindReference method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The FindReference method finds an object in the list of objects referenced by a Placeholder3D. It takes a Placement3D object (oObject) as a parameter and returns the index of the reference or -1 if the object was not found.

instruction: What does the FindVariable method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The FindVariable method finds a variable, specified by its name, within the Placeholder3D object. It takes a string strVarName (name of the variable) as a parameter and returns the index of the variable or -1 if not found.

instruction: What does the GetAllCurrentValues method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetAllCurrentValues method gets a list of all current values in the Placeholder3D object. It returns a PropertyValue list of all current values.

instruction: What does the GetCurrentValue method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetCurrentValue method gets the current value for a given object, property, and index within the Placeholder3D object. It takes a StorableObject (oObject), AnyPropertyId (oProperty), and an integer (oIndex) as parameters and returns a PropertyValue object or null.

instruction: What does the GetProjectPropertyEntry method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetProjectPropertyEntry method gets a value or variable of a Placeholder3D project property. It takes an AnyPropertyId (oProperty) as a parameter and returns the value of the variable.

instruction: What does the GetPropertyEntry method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetPropertyEntry method gets a value or variable on a property of an object referenced by a Placeholder3D. It takes a Placement3D object (oObject) and an AnyPropertyId (oProperty) as parameters and returns the value of the variable.

instruction: What does the GetRecordNames method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetRecordNames method returns the names of all records in the nIndex-th PlaceHolder3D of a Macro. It returns a StringCollection of record names.

instruction: What does the GetValue method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetValue method gets the value of a variable for a record within the Placeholder3D object. It takes a string strRecordName (the name of the record) and a string strVarName (the name of the variable) as parameters.

instruction: What does the GetValues method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetValues method returns the values of all variables in the nIndex-th PlaceHolder3D of a Macro variant for a given record. It takes a string strRecordName (record name) as a parameter and returns a StringCollection of the variable values.

instruction: What does the GetVariableNames method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The GetVariableNames method returns the names of all variables in the nIndex-th PlaceHolder3D of a Macro variant. It returns a StringCollection of variable names.

instruction: What does the IsVariableValid method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The IsVariableValid method verifies the correctness of a variable name of a placeholder. It returns false if the variable name contains invalid characters, and true otherwise. It takes a string strVariableName (variable name to check) as a parameter.

instruction: What exceptions can the IsVariableValid method throw?
output: The IsVariableValid method can throw System.ArgumentNullException, System.ArgumentException, System.ApplicationException, and Eplan.EplApi.Base.BaseException.

instruction: What does the RemoveInvalidObjectReferences method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The RemoveInvalidObjectReferences method removes invalid (e.g. deleted) object references from a PlaceHolder3D.

instruction: What does the RemoveObjectReferences method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The RemoveObjectReferences method removes object references from a PlaceHolder3D. It takes an array of StorableObject (pObjects) as a parameter.

instruction: What does the SetProjectPropertyEntry method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The SetProjectPropertyEntry method sets a value or variable on a Placeholder3D project property. The reference of the object will be added to the Placeholder3D if necessary. It takes an AnyPropertyId (oProperty) and a MultiLangString (strValue) as parameters.

instruction: What does the SetPropertyEntry method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The SetPropertyEntry method sets a value or variable on a property of an object referenced by a Placeholder3D. The reference of the object will be added to the Placeholder3D if necessary. It takes a Placement3D object (oObject), an AnyPropertyId (oProperty), and a MultiLangString (strValue) as parameters.

instruction: What does the SetValue method do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The SetValue method sets the value of a variable for a record within the Placeholder3D object. It takes a string strRecordName (the name of the record), a string strVarName (the name of the variable), and a MultiLangString (mlsValue) as parameters.

instruction: What is the purpose of the AssignedObjects property in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The AssignedObjects property gets or sets a list of StorableObject references to a PlaceHolder3D object. The originally assigned references are replaced.

instruction: What is the InstallationSpace property in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The InstallationSpace property returns the InstallationSpace in which this object exists or `null`.

instruction: What does the IsDocumentReferenceActive property do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The IsDocumentReferenceActive property activates the properties of the installation space for the place holder object. If true, then installation space properties will be displayed in the properties dialog.

instruction: What does the NameOfRecord property do in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The NameOfRecord property gets or sets the name of a record, specified by its index. The index is zero-based.

instruction: What is the NumberOfRecords property in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The NumberOfRecords property returns the count of records.

instruction: What is the NumberOfReferences property in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The NumberOfReferences property returns the count of objects referenced by the Placeholder3D.

instruction: What is the NumberOfVariables property in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The NumberOfVariables property returns the count of variables.

instruction: What is the Value property in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The Value property gets or sets the value of a variable for a record. It takes the record name and variable name as parameters.

instruction: What is the VariableNames property in the PlaceHolder3D class?
output: The VariableNames property returns the names of all variables in the Placeholder3D. It returns a StringCollection of variable names.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PlaceHolder3DPropertyList constructor that takes a PlaceHolder3D object?
output: The PlaceHolder3DPropertyList constructor that takes a PlaceHolder3D object should be used internally only. It initializes the property list for a specific PlaceHolder3D object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Property(AnyPropertyId) property in PlaceHolder3DPropertyList?
output: The Property(AnyPropertyId) property is a method used by the operator[] to access properties by AnyPropertyId. It returns a PropertyValue object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32) property in PlaceHolder3DPropertyList?
output: The Property(AnyPropertyId, Int32) property is a method used by the operator[] to access indexed properties by AnyPropertyId. It returns a PropertyValue object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Property(PlaceHolder) property in PlaceHolder3DPropertyList?
output: The Property(PlaceHolder) property is a method used by the operator[] to access properties. It returns a PropertyValue object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Property(PlaceHolder, Int32) property in PlaceHolder3DPropertyList?
output: The Property(PlaceHolder, Int32) property is a method used by the operator[] to access indexed properties. It returns a PropertyValue object.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PLACEHOLDER_DESCRIPTION property in PlaceHolderPropertyList?
output: The PLACEHOLDER_DESCRIPTION property represents the description of the placeholder object. It returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PLACEHOLDER_LASTUSEDVALUESET property in PlaceHolderPropertyList?
output: The PLACEHOLDER_LASTUSEDVALUESET property represents the value set that was last assigned to the placeholder object. It returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PLACEHOLDER_NAME property in PlaceHolderPropertyList?
output: The PLACEHOLDER_NAME property represents the name of the placeholder object. It returns a property value of type System.String. This property is read-only.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PLACEHOLDER_USE_IN_COGINEER property in PlaceHolderPropertyList?
output: The PLACEHOLDER_USE_IN_COGINEER property indicates whether this placeholder object can be used in Eplan Cogineer. It returns a property value of type System.Boolean. This property is read-only.

instruction: What is the purpose of the PLACEHOLDER_VALUESET_NAME property in PlaceHolderPropertyList?
output: The PLACEHOLDER_VALUESET_NAME property represents the value set name. It returns a property value of type System.String. This property is read-only and indexed, with possible indexes from 1 to 100.

instruction: What does the ResetFilter method do in the PlaceHolders3DFilter class?
output: The ResetFilter method resets the filter. After the reset, DMObjectsFinder will return all Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.E3D.PlaceHolder3Ds in the project.

instruction: What does the Name property represent in the PlaceHolders3DFilter class?
output: The Name property represents the name of the objects which will be used to filter objects.

instruction: What are the parameters of the Create(Page, String, Double) method?
output: The Create(Page, String, Double) method takes three parameters: page (the Page the Text will be placed on), contents (a string containing the text to display), and textHeight (the height of the Text).

instruction: What exceptions can the Create(Page, String, Double) method throw?
output: The Create(Page, String, Double) method can throw the following exceptions: System.ArgumentNullException, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectCreationException, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.ObjectAlreadyCreatedException, Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.IncorrectObjectTypeException, and Eplan.EplApi.DataModel.InvalidArgumentException.

instruction: What is the purpose of the ActionName property in the PlaceHolderText class?
output: The ActionName property gets or sets the action name.

instruction: What is the GroupType property in the PlaceHolderText class?
output: The GroupType property represents the group type.

instruction: What is the PlaceHolderType property in the PlaceHolderText class?
output: The PlaceHolderType property represents the placeholder type.

instruction: What is the PropDescr property in the PlaceHolderText class?
output: The PropDescr property represents the property Id and index.

instruction: What is the SourceObject property in the PlaceHolderText class?
output: The SourceObject property gets an object from which a PlaceHolderText was created.

instruction: What are the parameters of the CopyTo(Group, Boolean, Boolean) method?
output: The CopyTo(Group, Boolean, Boolean) method takes three parameters: destinationGroup (Group or Page, where the placement will be inserted), bMatchLayerNames (defines whether a layer should be matched by name), and bFixUserDefinedDisplayedProperties (defines whether user-defined property definitions should be matched by identifying name).

instruction: What does the GetBoundingBox method do in the Placement class?
output: The GetBoundingBox method returns the placement bounding box. The bounding box is a rectangle that contains the placement and can be used to determine the placement size.

instruction: What does the Remove method do in the Placement class?
output: The Remove method removes the placement.

instruction: What are the parameters of the Scale(Double, Double, PointD) method?
output: The Scale(Double, Double, PointD) method takes three parameters: fXAxis (X axis factor), fYAxis (Y axis factor), and ptOrigin (scaling origin point).

instruction: What does the DrawingOrder property do in the Placement class?
output: The DrawingOrder property sets the display sequence. The drawing order flag will be used to sort elements according to drawing order within a group. If elements share the same value, the drawing order will result from the order of the data model. The default value is 67.

instruction: What does the Group property do in the Placement class?
output: The Group property returns a group that the Placement object belongs to. If the Placement object doesn't belong to any group, NULL is returned.

instruction: What does the IsPlaced property do in the Placement class?
output: The IsPlaced property returns true if the placement is placed.

instruction: What does the Location property do in the Placement class?
output: The Location property gets or sets the placement's location.

instruction: What does the Page property do in the Placement class?
output: The Page property returns the page the Placement is on, or assigns a Page object to the placement. If the placement was previously assigned to another page, it is removed from the old one and assigned to the page given as an argument.

instruction: What does the Properties property do in the Placement class?
output: The Properties property exposes P8 properties.

instruction: What does the Tooltip property do in the Placement class?
output: The Tooltip property gets the tooltips of the object as can be seen in the GED.

instruction: What does the AddConnectionPointPosition method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The AddConnectionPointPosition method creates a new connection point position.

instruction: What does the AddMatePersistent method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The AddMatePersistent method adds a copy of a mate to a Placement3D. It takes a Mate object (pCustomMate) as a parameter.

instruction: What exceptions can the AddMatePersistent method throw?
output: The AddMatePersistent method can throw System.ArgumentNullException.

instruction: What does the CopyTo(Placement3D) method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The CopyTo(Placement3D) method copies the 3D Placement and its children into the same Project as newParent. Then, the parent of the copy will be set to newParent. It takes a Placement3D object (newParent) as a parameter.

instruction: What does the FindSourceMate(String, PlacementOptions, Boolean) method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The FindSourceMate(String, PlacementOptions, Boolean) method finds a source mate by name. It takes a string name (mate name), a Mate.Enums.PlacementOptions (ePlacementOptions), and a boolean bConsiderMountingSurfaces (consider child mounting surfaces) as parameters.

instruction: What does the FindTargetMate(String, Boolean, Boolean) method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The FindTargetMate(String, Boolean, Boolean) method finds a target mate by name. It takes a string name (mate name), a boolean bConsiderMountingClearance (consider mounting clearance), and a boolean bConsiderMountingSurfaces (consider child mounting surfaces) as parameters.

instruction: What does the GetBoundingBox method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The GetBoundingBox method returns the bounding box of a Placement3D. It takes a boolean bWithChildren as a parameter, which indicates whether to consider children.

instruction: What does the GetBoundingBoxRelative method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The GetBoundingBoxRelative method returns the relative bounding box of a Placement3D.

instruction: What does the GetSourceMates(PlacementOptions, Boolean) method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The GetSourceMates(PlacementOptions, Boolean) method gets an array of all source mates from this object. It takes a Mate.Enums.PlacementOptions (ePlacementOptions) and a boolean bConsiderMountingSurfaces (consider child mounting surfaces) as parameters.

instruction: What does the GetTargetMates(Boolean, Boolean) method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The GetTargetMates(Boolean, Boolean) method gets an array of all target mates from this object. It takes a boolean bConsiderMountingClearance (consider mounting clearance) and a boolean bConsiderMountingSurfaces (consider child mounting surfaces) as parameters.

instruction: What does the Move method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The Move method moves this object. It takes three double parameters: x, y, and z, which represent the movement vector.

instruction: What does the MoveRelative method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The MoveRelative method moves this object relative to its parent. It takes three double parameters: x, y, and z, which represent the movement vector.

instruction: What does the Remove method do in the Placement3D class?
output: The Remove method removes the object from the project and all its children.

instruction: What does the FUNC_ADDITIONALIDENTIFYINGNAMEPART property contain?
output: The FUNC_ADDITIONALIDENTIFYINGNAMEPART property contains the terminal designation for terminals, the pin designation for pins, and the connection point designation for connection points.

instruction: What is the format of the values in the FUNC_ALLCONNECTIONCROSSSECTIONS property?
output: During entering (in the property dialog or for tabular editing) the individual values are separated by paragraph marks. For the display the values are separated by semicolons.

instruction: What happens if you set the FUNC_ALLCONNECTIONCROSSSECTIONS property with an invalid value?
output: When setting the property an exception is thrown, if no value of the semicolon separated string is saved. This means the object has connection points (Pins) or the string is empty.

instruction: How are the connection point descriptions separated in the FUNC_ALLCONNECTIONDESCRIPTIONS property?
output: During entering (in the property dialog or for tabular editing) the individual values are separated by paragraph marks. For the display the values are separated by semicolons.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_ALLPLACEMENTLOCATIONS property?
output: The FUNC_ALLPLACEMENTLOCATIONS property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What is the data type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_ABSORPTION_VOLUME property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_ABSORPTION_VOLUME property returns a property value of type System.String.

instruction: What does the FUNC_ARTICLE_ACCURACY_FOR_DYNAMIC_VISCOSITY property describe?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_ACCURACY_FOR_DYNAMIC_VISCOSITY property describes the precision with which a device or system can measure the dynamic viscosity of a liquid. This specification is often expressed as a percentage (%) and indicates how accurate the measurements are compared to the actual value.

instruction: What does the FUNC_ARTICLE_ACCURACY_FOR_OPERATING_VOLUME_FLOW_RATE property describe?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_ACCURACY_FOR_OPERATING_VOLUME_FLOW_RATE property describes the precision with which a device or system can measure the flow rate of an operating volume. This specification is often expressed as a percentage (%) and indicates how accurate the measurements are compared to the actual flow rate.

instruction: What is the unit of measurement for active power?
output: Active power is measured in watt (W).

instruction: What is the unit of measurement for active power loss?
output: Active power loss is measured in watt (W).

instruction: What is the unit of measurement for apparent power?
output: Apparent power is measured in volt amperes (VA).

instruction: What is the return type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_APPLICATION_AREA_OF_THE_CABLE property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_APPLICATION_AREA_OF_THE_CABLE property returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the return type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_APPLICATION_RANGE_OF_THE_CONNECTION_CABLE property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_APPLICATION_RANGE_OF_THE_CONNECTION_CABLE property returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is an assembly?
output: An assembly is a collection of parts that belong to a device (e.g. a pushbutton with a normally open contact, the appropriate mounting and the button). An assembly has its own part number and can also contain (sub) assemblies.

instruction: What is the return type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_BACNET property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_BACNET property returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What does the FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_ENTRY_INTO_THE_DEVICE property specify?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_ENTRY_INTO_THE_DEVICE property specifies the way in which a cable is inserted and fastened into the device, for example by means of a screw connection.

instruction: What is the return type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_LEVEL property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_LEVEL property returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the return type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_PIPE_TRANSMITTER_CONNECTION property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_PIPE_TRANSMITTER_CONNECTION property returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the return type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_WINDER property?
output: The FUNC_ARTICLE_CABLE_WINDER property returns a property value of type Eplan.EplApi.Base.MultiLangString.

instruction: What is the unit of measurement for CO2 emission?
output: The emission data for air emissions are specified in the unit E3 kg/TJ. They can be converted into the more common unit g/kWh by multiplying by the factor 3.6.

instruction: What is the return type of the FUNC_ARTICLE_CONNECTABLE_CABLE_TYPE property?
